observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Accountant
and the Property and Supply Officer to reconcile the records, investigate any
discrepancies, and make the necessary procedures in the disposition of
missing PPEs to ensure the accurate reflection of the PPE account balances.

We recommended that Management efficiently and effectively monitor the
recoupment of advances made to contractors, ensuring that these are repaid
accordingly and likewise initiate the procedures on the forfeiture of
performance bond posted by the defaulting contractors in case of non-
completion, delay or termination of contracts.

We recommended and Management agreed that, through the City
Engineering Office and City Planning and Development Office and other
concerned offices such as the end-users, to formulate collectively a
comprehensive and realistic plan to expedite the procurement and
implementation of all identified priority development projects under the 20
per cent DF.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRMO and the
LDRRMC:
a. Integrate the separate LDRRMFIP into the regular AIP to streamline the
management and allocation of funds while ensuring transparency and
accountability in the utilization of the LDRRMF;
b. Foster closer coordination and communication between the LDRRMO and
the departments responsible for the Annual Investment Plan to align the
objectives of the separate LDRRMFIP with the overall goals and priorities
outlined in the regular AIP;
c. Establish an efficient monitoring system to ensure the separate
LDRRMFIP complies with the requirements outlined in COA Circular No.
2012-002;
d. Provide training and capacity-building initiatives for the LDRRMO staff
and other personnel involved in preparing and implementing the
LDRRMFIP and the AIP; and
e. Maintain a comprehensive and organized documentation of all LDRRMF
documents to ensure that accurate and timely reports are submitted to
the appropriate offices.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRMO adhere to
the provisions outlined in NDRRMC-MC NO. 64, s. 2021 on the protection
of ACDVs so that the LDRRMF would only be used for its intended purpose.

We recommended that Management conduct a comprehensive operational
review of the Operation of the Waterworks System to become self-reliant
and avoid losses so that it can contribute to sustainable development and
community empowerment.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Enforce the terms and conditions of the contract, particularly on full
delivery requirements before payment, unless partial delivery is
explicitly authorized and clearly provided for and stipulated in the
contract, including delivery and payment schedules;
b. Impose liquidated damages, in accordance with the contract and RA
9184, in all instances of delayed or incomplete delivery, unless a valid
reason supported by documentation is accepted and approved by the
HOPE; and
c. Develop and adopt standard guidelines on the handling of partial
deliveries and payments to ensure consistency, legal compliance, and
alignment with government procurement principles of transparency,
efficiency, and accountability.

We recommended that Management enforce compliance with the terms and
specifications stated in the contract and the Request for Quotation (RFQ),
such that items delivered exactly match the descriptions, brand names, and
specifications indicated therein, unless a formal amendment of the contract is
duly signed by both parties prior to delivery.

We recommended and Management agreed to secure the DOH-LTO for the
five vehicles marked with “AMBULANCE” otherwise, register them as
Patient Transport Vehicle (PTV) and change the labels accordingly pursuant
DOH AO No. 2018-001 dated January 28, 2018.

We reiterated our recommendation if management can be used by the City for its intended purpose that would ultimately redound to the benefit of all constituents of the city. Ensure its proper maintenance to prevent deterioration and to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary and high costs of repair.

We recommended that the City Engineer provide the City Accountant with copies of the report on completed projects, together with the certificates of completion and acceptance, to support the accounting entry to close the CIP accounts to the appropriate infrastructure asset accounts.
We further recommended that the City Accountant compute the applicable depreciation expense to arrive at an accurate and reliable carrying balance of the PPE, pursuant to Section 50 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the PPE Inventory Committee strictly observe the procedural guidelines on the conduct of a physical count of PPE and follow the appropriate numbering system, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
We further recommended that the City Government comply with Sections 7.0 and 8.0 of the Circular on the disposition of non-existing-or-missing PPEs and the derecognition of these PPEs without an available record or accountability to fast-track the availment of the one-time cleansing of non-existing-or-missing PPE items to ensure that reported PPE balances are verifiable as to existence, condition, and accountability.

We further recommended that henceforth, the City Engineer closely monitor any project implemented by contract to ensure that the contractor works on and completes the projects within the contract period and in accordance with specifications.

We recommended that the City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) adequately and thoroughly plan all infrastructure projects before their inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) to ensure that only those which are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready are included therein pursuant to Section 3.2.3 of the DBM, DOF, and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2010.
We further recommended that the CPDO also agreed to closely monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different PPAs of the City Government so that the intended benefits could be enjoyed by the constituents.
We recommended that in the preparation of the 20 per cent DF annual budget, the projects and activities to be funded are to be identified or specified.

We recommended that the LDRRMC and LDRRMO review and update the LDRRMFIP annually to include projects and activities chargeable to prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF in accordance with Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, to maximize the use of the fund to ensure that appropriate measures in disaster risk reduction and management are implemented.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2014 to 2016 LDRRMF unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund for adoption by the Sangguniang Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for other social services.

We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned department heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the local roads asset management system and faithfully perform their respective duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated October 23, 2015.
We further recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an annual physical count of all LRN and the results thereof reported in the RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than January 31 of each year.
We also recommended that the result thereof shall be reconciled with the accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries are prepared before the statement date.

We recommended that the City Agriculturist conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and render a report thereon, including the determination of the fair market value of each stock, to the City Accountant, for proper recording in the books of accounts, and henceforth, he must keep a systematic record of births, deaths and disposals of breeding stocks. In case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request for relief from accountability and for the dropping of the account from the books in accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
We further recommended that Management adhere to the provisions of IPSAS No. 27 by updating the breeding stocks account balance and record gains and losses through changes in fair market values at every reporting period.

We recommended that Management thoroughly assess the cost-effectiveness of the financing scheme so that unnecessary interest payments will be avoided.
We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee and the City Development Council to review the feasibility and relevance of development projects funded by prior years’ 20% Development Fund which have remained unimplemented to date and realign these projects as well as the balances of completed projects to finance other currently-needed priority development projects.

We recommended that Management require the City Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) and the City Engineer to regularly monitor the completion of the projects and impose sanctions and penalties, if warranted, for the delay in the completion thereof pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
We further recommended that proper planning, monitoring and efficient implementation of projects be done by the CPDO and City Engineer so as to achieve the desired results and avoid voluminous unimplemented projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Treasurer to verify from their own records or the records of the BIR and other sources the accuracy of the taxpayers’ declared gross receipts in order to assess the correct amount of tax.
Moreover, we recommended and Management also agreed to require the City Treasurer to send letters of collection to concerned suppliers/taxpayers for the tax deficiency.

We recommended that Management formulate guidelines and criteria on the hiring of individuals under Contract of Service in accordance with CSC, COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017.

We recommended that the City conduct an inventory of its insurable properties giving priority to high-value items, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund and insure the same with the GIF of the GSIS every year.

We recommended that management facilitate the approval of its staffing pattern and immediately employ and/or appoint an educationally qualified and properly motivated person to discharge the functions of a General Services Officer in pursuance of the Local Government Code of 1991.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management enjoin the concerned department
heads to strictly comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the
local roads asset management system and faithfully perform their respective
duties and responsibilities set forth under COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated
October 23, 2015.

We further recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct an
annual physical count of all LRN and the results thereof reported in the
RPCLRN to be submitted to the Auditor and Accounting unit not later than
January 31 of each year.

We also recommended that result thereof shall be reconciled with the
accounting records, and the necessary adjusting entries are prepared before
the statement date.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Agriculturist
conduct an inventory of breeding stocks and render a report thereon,
including the determination of the fair market value of each stock, to the City
Accountant, for proper recording in the books of accounts, and henceforth, he
must keep a systematic record of births, deaths and disposals of breeding
stocks. In case of death or disposal, he shall facilitate the request for relief
from accountability and for the dropping the account from the books in
accordance with Sections 151 and 152 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated
October 20, 1992.

We further recommended and Management greed to adhere to the
provisions of IPSAS No. 27 by updating the breeding stocks account balance
and record gains and losses through changes in fair market values at every
reporting period.

We recommended and Management manifested its intention to ensure
that detailed description of the services to be rendered or activities to be
performed be indicated in the Job Order contracts for repairs and
maintenance so that validity and propriety of the payment of wages could be
evaluated. Moreover, Management must evaluate its policies in hiring job
order workers and be guided by the rules and regulation provided for under
CSC-COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 1, dated June 15, 2017.

We recommended that Management thoroughly assess the cost
effectiveness of financing scheme so that unnecessary interest payments will
be avoided. We further recommended that the City Mayor direct the Local
Finance Committee and the City Development Council to review the feasibility
and relevance of development projects funded by prior years’ 20%
Development Fund which have remained unimplemented to date and realign
these projects as well as the balances of completed projects to finance other
currently-needed priority development projects.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management refrain from making
initial payments on purchases of lots unless specifically authorized by law or
regulations. Moreover, secure the Certificate of Title for the lot purchased in
December 2018 so that lot ownership belongs to the City and its interest is fully
protected.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to maintain a
special account for loans availed from Land Bank of the Philippines with the
authority by the Sangguniang Panlungsod in order that assets acquired and
the interest expenses incurred therein can be easily monitored.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City
Planning and Development Officer (CPDO) and the City Engineer to regularly
monitor the completion of the projects and impose sanctions and penalties, if
warranted, for the delay in the completion thereof pursuant to the pertinent
provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act
No. 9184. We further recommended that proper planning, monitoring and
efficient implementation of projects be done by the CPDO and City Engineer
so as to achieve the desired results and avoid voluminous unimplemented
projects.

We recommended and the City Mayor agreed to direct the City
Treasurer to verify from their own records or the records of the BIR and other
sources the accuracy of the taxpayers’ declared gross receipts in order to
assess the correct amount of tax. Moreover, we recommended and
Management also agreed to require the City Treasurer to send letters of
collection to concerned suppliers/taxpayers for the tax deficiency.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to transfer project
expenditures under the Trust Fund to the General Fund at year-end or upon
project completion, whichever comes first, so that the accounts in the financial
statements will be fairly presented.

observations.reason: Some of the beneficiaries listed under the UCT program are already deceased or are no longer qualified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management formulate guidelines and criteria on the hiring of Contract of Service (COS) in accordance with CSC, COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The City Mayor issued an Order prescribing the guidelines in the hiring of Contract of Service (COS) in accordance with CSC, COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated June 15, 2017. The City reduced its COS workers to 87.
observations.reason: Some Contract of Service workers have the same tasks/accomplishment as that of the Job Order workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare a one-time report to the NDRRMC, through OCD on all donations received by the City in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 and furnish the Auditor a copy of the report together with the Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind; Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The City submitted report on donations received and distributed in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020. Distribution Sheets of the donations received were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the CPDO and the City Engineer to coordinate for the preparation and submission of the list of PPAs at the beginning of the year as well as quarterly reports so that all projects/programs/activities of the City will be included therein.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The CPDO and the City Engineer submitted quarterly reports listing projects for the year and will ensure the inclusion of all PPAs in the lists.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that procurement of refrigerators, chest freezers and communication expenses be sourced from the General Fund of the LGU and refund the LDRRMF for the amount expended. Further, food and transportation expenses (including fuel) of medical and other LGU personnel directly involved in the implementation of COVID-19 related PPAs, and other related expenses shall be charged to BGCM and 20% DF during the State of Calamity as declared by the President.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 The City refunded the LDRRMF in the amount of ₱2,345,062.00 on August 12, 2021. The City will source future related expenses from the General Fund or other appropriate funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management fast-track the completion of the projects, require the City Engineer to monitor the completion of the projects and impose sanctions and penalties, where warranted, for the delay in the completion thereof pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The City completed two out of the eight projects. The City Engineer is monitoring the projects and will impose sanctions where warranted.
observations.reason: Peace and order situation of the City and the difficulty in transporting materials due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the rules and regulations on the use of government vehicles be strictly requiring, for each and every trip, a trip ticket which contains all the information required and for the odometers of all vehicles to be working properly and filled up before being approved.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018, information in 2013 the trip tickets were already implemented and odometers of some vehicles are functioning. Passengers sign for certification as to official use.
observations.reason: Odometers of 23 vehicles are not functioning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC consolidate its requisitions and procure its diesel and procure its needs through public bidding and avail of bulk discounts and be shielded from constant increase in the prices of fuel.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018 The BAC called for public bidding and procured diesel and gasoline twice but no prospective bidder participated in the bidding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management hire more Engineering personnel to prepare the required documents for the implementation of projects and the City Development Council review the feasibility and relevance of prior years’ 20% Development Fund projects which have remained unimplemented to be realigned or given priority.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2018, 2017, 2016 Approved creation of four positions. Appropriation Ordinance 2021-34-A.
observations.reason: A long process in the creation and hiring of positions; budget consideration is needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management revise the conditions set forth in the Deed of Undertaking to ensure grantees repay their loans whether or not the animals survived; require the City Agriculturist to regularly monitor and inspect the status of swine in the hands of loan grantees; require the City Accountant to recognize loan receivables for loans granted on the basis of the Deeds of Undertaking; and stop the practice of charging purchases directly to expense and instead implement prescribed procedures.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The City conducted monitoring and reconciled records. Booked Loans Receivable for the Swine Feeds Program granted in 2014 and 2015 amounting to ₱13,074,118.80 and loan collections amounting to ₱2,760,484.60 were recorded in January 2018. Institutionalized use of RIS in issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing records from the barangays and the volume of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Human Resource Management Officer carefully assess the necessity of hiring job order workers against filling up available vacant permanent positions and ensure job order tasks do not duplicate regular employee functions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 To address the deeper problem of poverty and unemployment, some jobs which do not require a high level of skill were rotated to different workers so that more workers can be hired with no additional budget. Accomplishment reports were attached to the payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Legal Office take concrete steps for the immediate recovery of the advance payments made to the contractors and for forfeiture of the performance and surety bonds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Of the three projects recommended for recovery of advance payments, one met misfortune after another and still failed to comply. The City is studying the steps for immediate recovery and collection of claims.
observations.reason: One contractor unfortunately met misfortune and was unable to comply with the demand.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor create a Disposal Committee which shall take charge of the disposal of the unserviceable properties following COA Circular No. 92-386 and that the General Services Officer prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) using the NGAS-prescribed format so that important information such as cost will be indicated.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 Created Disposal Committee to take charge of disposal of unserviceable properties. Submitted to the Audit Team the existing Schedule of Unserviceable Property and Equipment for disposal.
observations.reason: Determination of the appraised value of the unserviceable properties is pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of wastes; acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at source and point of collection; widen the coverage of the city’s garbage collection system; and require the SWM Board to strengthen their IEC Program to increase awareness of constituents on waste management.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The City collects ₱500.00 per month from each official and employee who received PEI in CY 2014. The City already acquired a lot in 2017 to be developed as the City’s sanitary landfill. The City sent electronic file of its Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan to DENR-EMB on December 2019. The City submitted its 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan.
observations.reason: Needs partnership of the barangays on the Information, Education and Communication (IEC) in waste segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and avoid repercussions that may result from its non-utilization of BUB funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2017 The BUB program projects were fast-tracked and progress monitored. Out of nine projects, seven were completed.
observations.reason: Technical capacity constraints and implementation issues caused delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive require the conduct of the physical inventory count and submission of a report thereon to the audit team and that results be reconciled with accounting and property records with necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 Physical inventory was conducted and results reconciled with accounting records. Inventory count was conducted by the inventory team and results were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Require the City Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and update monthly using the aggregating reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and require the GSO to maintain property cards; b) Require the City Accountant to reconcile records on work/zoo animals and breeding stocks with those maintained by the City Agriculturist and draw the JEV for reported births or drop from the record in case of death/transfer/sale; and c) Require the City Agriculturist to prepare a report within two days from the birth or death of animals and prepare Monthly Report of Inventory.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The Office of the City Accountant maintained Livestock Ledger Cards while the GSO conducted a physical inventory of livestock in the first quarter of CY 2021. The City conducted reconciliations and instituted monthly reporting.
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing the records especially for those issued by the GSO more than 10 years ago.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the immediate implementation of the BUB programs so that the benefits expected from the implementation will not be delayed further.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The three projects were prioritized and immediate implementation was pursued. Projects were completed where feasible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Concretize methods and procedures for the eventual closure of the existing open dumpsite within a specific time frame; b) Endeavor to operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site and acquire/develop a suitable lot; c) Set a time frame for the City Solid Waste Management Board to formulate a new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan incorporating waste segregation and collection systems; and d) Strengthen IEC Program.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2016 The dumpsite was closed on March 30, 2021. The City acquired a lot in 2017 for sanitary landfill development and submitted its 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan to DENR-EMB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund and insure the City properties with the GSIS GIF every year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The Mega Market was insured by the City. The City continues to prioritize insurable properties for coverage.
observations.reason: The City prioritized the Mega Market which was insured; other properties pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Assessor to prepare the SMV pursuant to Section 212 of the Local Government Code and cause the passage of an Ordinance incorporating the SMV as basis for the conduct of a general revision of real property assessments.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The City is studying the proposed schedule of market values (SMV) and working to come up with the full schedule. The City assessor office returned for revision and is working to cause the passage of the SMV ordinance.
observations.reason: The City still has no approved SMV ordinance; staff are working to finalize schedule.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending government funds on entertainment which do not benefit the City and to refund the amount disbursed as reflected in Notices of Disallowance issued by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The City discontinued the practice and will refund the amounts where Notices of Disallowance were issued and will monitor settlement. Notices of Disallowance were issued in CY 2015 as basis for monitoring.
observations.reason: The City has difficulty in getting refunds from private individuals or entities; some recovery actions are in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Submit the required documents for negotiated procurement including BAC Resolution; b) Require the CDRRMO and CSWDO to account for distribution of said relief items and submit the distribution list of beneficiaries with addresses and acknowledgement receipts; and c) Submit written explanation on inconsistencies between date of distribution and date of acceptance and inspection of the items.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Some supporting documents were retrieved. The CDRRMO and CSWDO assured the Audit Team that distribution lists and beneficiary acknowledgment were submitted where possible. The former CDRRMO and CSWDO personnel have retired; the City is instituting better record-keeping.
observations.reason: Some vouchers/documents could not be located due to constant relocation of files and flooding of the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all city officials and employees to refund the overpayment of PEI and observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The City collects ₱500.00 per month from each official and employee who received PEI in CY 2014. Notices of Disallowance were issued and collections for CY 2014 are being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of disbursements, and henceforth observe strictly the policies and procedures in the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 in procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The City retrieved some of the vouchers and is using the DBM-PS list where items are available; purchases for items not available from PS-DBM were justified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Accountant to review and investigate the difference by tracing the erroneous journal entries, prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts and henceforth regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts to ensure the fairness of presentation.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 The City Accountant reconciled the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts in the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books and these accounts are already reconciled as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel responsible for processing payments for gasoline and oil be required to restitute the amount and that the City Accountant and accounting personnel be instructed to approve payment for just the quantity actually delivered.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Demand Letter was already issued to the supplier. Processing of payments for fuel are now being handled with meticulous care.
observations.reason: Refund was not made by the supplier; follow-up actions continue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate the approval of its staffing pattern and immediately employ and/or appoint an educationally-qualified and properly motivated person to discharge the functions of a General Services Officer in pursuance of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The creation of the position was approved on September 14, 2016. The City is still in process of filling the position.
observations.reason: The City still has to hire a full-time GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant thoroughly review the validity of those long outstanding payable accounts, remit to individuals/agencies concerned like the GSIS, PAG-IBIG, other LGUs those payables found to be valid, and request for reversion back to surplus all those which have remained outstanding in the books for more than two years and for which no administrative or judicial claim has been filed. Also recommended strict compliance with regulations in recognition and recording of liabilities and periodic review and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2009 The City Accountant thoroughly reviews payable accounts and remits to concerned agencies where valid. The City reconciled payables and reversed accounts payable outstanding for more than two years where no claim existed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee
to conduct a physical inventory count of the City’s PPE and submit a report
thereon to the Audit Team. Moreover, the results of the actual physical
inventory should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and
the necessary adjustments made so that both records agree with the actual
count in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City
Accountant and the City Budget Officer to record the expenses of the City to
its appropriate specific account provided under COA Circular No. 2015-009
dated December 01, 2015 so that a more precise budgetary control could be
put in place and proper recording could be made.

We recommended that Management account for the unaccounted items
procured as relief goods distributed to the constituents of the City and submit
master lists of the beneficiaries for all the barangays, as well as, a written
explanation on the alterations of the dates of distribution found in the Relief
Distribution Sheets.

We recommended that the City transact directly with legally,
technically and financially capable suppliers of known qualifications as
provided for under GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020 dated March 9, 2020 in
order to avail of the most advantageous prices with corresponding warranty.

We recommended that the Peace and Order Council evaluate the
programs, activities and projects under its Peace and Order Plan to align with
Section 3.23 of Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND so
that the exact amount for Confidential Fund will be appropriated accordingly.

We recommended that Management require the requisitioner of the food
items purchased to submit the daily menu for meals served for persons under
monitoring to the Audit Team in order for the latter to validate the usage
thereof. Moreover, we recommended that the end user and the BAC adhere
strictly to Section 7.3.2 (f) of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards
Committee to submit justification to prove reasonableness for the
procurement of the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH.
Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the SRP
set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most
advantageous to the government.

We recommended that Management establish a system in the
distribution of SAP and similar cash assistance programs where proper
validation and appropriate profiling of beneficiaries are performed before
disbursement. We further recommended that the CSWDO apprise the DSWD
Regional Office that some of the beneficiaries listed under the Unconditional
Cash Transfer (UCT) program are already deceased or are no longer
qualified. Lastly, we recommended that Management adhere to the DSWD
Memorandum Circular No. 09 series of 2020 requirements in the
implementation of social amelioration programs and other similar programs
of DSWD to ensure that the assistance are given to intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and Management agreed to formulate guidelines
and criteria on the hiring of individuals under Contract of Service in
accordance with CSC, COA and DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017 dated June
15, 2017.

We recommended and Management agreed to prepare a one-time
report to the National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council
(NDRRMC), through the Office of the Civil Defense (OCD) on all donations
received by the City in accordance with Section II of COA Circular No. 2020-
009 dated April 21, 2020 and furnish the Auditor a copy of the report together
with the following:
(1) Acknowledgement Receipts of the Donations In-Kind;
(2) Proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries; and
(3) Inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the CPDO and
the City Engineer to coordinate with each other in the preparation and
submission of the list of PPAs at the beginning of the year as well as the
quarterly reports so that all projects/programs/activities of the City will be
included therein.

We recommended that the City consolidate its fuel requisitions and procure its needs through public bidding as required under RA No. 9184 and its IRR in order that it can avail of the most advantageous prices and of bulk discounts, if any, and also be shielded from the constant increase in the prices of fuel.
observations.managementAction: The BAC stopped purchasing fuel through negotiated procurement and requested assistance that the availability of a storage facility for fuel could still not be done due to lack of storage facility for fuel.
observations.reason: Management will still wait for availability of a storage facility for fuel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of disbursements and henceforth observe the policies and procedures under the revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction: The PS-DBM office is located in Cebu City; procurement from common-use suppliers would entail additional costs (freight, courier, fuel) and the City evaluated procurement approaches.
observations.reason: Procurement from PS-DBM entails additional costs and logistical constraints; therefore purchases were from local suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management find ways to utilize the slaughterhouse to serve its intended purpose and ensure its proper maintenance to prevent deterioration.
observations.managementAction: The slaughterhouse is subject of an investigation being conducted by the Ombudsman; management to determine ways to utilize.
observations.reason: Subject of ongoing investigation by the Ombudsman; utilization pending outcome.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management submit explanations for the perceived
defects, require the City Engineer to monitor the completion of the projects and impose
sanctions and penalties, where warranted, for the delay in the completion thereof
pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184. We further recommended and the City
Accountant agreed to ensure that payments for these projects are subjected to liquidated
damages.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to require all PHWs
to support their claims for hazard pay with proof of actual exposure to specific
health hazards and occupational risks with certifications from appropriate agencies
and the documents required under Section 5.8 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated June 14, 2012 in order to establish validity of payments.

We recommended the reversion of undocumented accounts payable in order to
release funds for other priority purposes. We further recommended that henceforth,
the City Accountant ensure that only payables which are duly supported with the
required documents are recorded in the books of account pursuant to existing
regulations.

We recommended that Management exercise prudence in the determination of the
amount of mobile telephone allowance, taking into consideration the current call and
text plans offered by local cellular phone companies, and thus consider amending its
ordinance. We further recommended that the grant of said allowance be limited to
its organic personnel in view of the lack of legal basis to support the grant to national
officials stationed in the LGU.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Panlungsod pass an ordinance
establishing the guidelines and criteria for availment of the scholarship
program, particularly as to the income level of an applicant’s family, the
composition of the City Scholarship Committee, the screening process and
documentary requirements required of scholars (classified into new and
continuing), and consider requiring successful graduates to render service to
the city government, with pay for a fixed term in order that the city may reap
some benefits from its substantial expenses under the program. We further
recommended that the current roster of LGU scholars be reviewed in order to
determine their eligibility.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council pass a resolution reverting the unutilized unspent balances to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund for the years 2010 – 2013 to be adopted by the Sangguniang Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for other social services.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRM Council prepared a resolution reverting the unutilized balances of the LDRRMF for the years 2010 – 2013 to the General Fund thru the Local Development Council. The AAR recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent reappropriation for other social services.
observations.reason: The CDRRM Council resolution to revert the unutilized balances of the LDRRMF was not forwarded to the Sangguniang Panlungsod for approval.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the rules and regulations on the use of government vehicles be strictly implemented, requiring, for each and every trip, a trip ticket which contains all the required information such as the plate number, distance travelled, time of departure and arrival, name and signature of passenger, if any, thus precluding the auditor from determining whether vehicles were used only for official purpose, whether fuel was issued only to government vehicles, and whether fuel consumption amounting to ₱26,384,449.10 for CY 2018 was reasonable and not excessive.
observations.managementAction: Most of the trip tickets were already filled up except for some odometer readings of some vehicles. The information in the trip tickets contained all the required information before it is approved, by limiting vehicle use to official purposes only, by assigning personnel to monitor the reasonableness of fuel consumption, by ensuring that the odometers of all vehicles are working properly, and by requiring passengers to sign certification as to official use.
observations.reason: Odometers of 23 vehicles are not functioning.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant take up payments for the occupancy electricity consumption of occupants of the Mega Market as trust liabilities in the special account Market Operations and to charge to this account the payments to the electric service provider, in order to fairly present the assets, liabilities and cumulative results of operations.
observations.managementAction: Payments of electricity by occupants of the Mega Market were immediately taken up as trust liabilities in the special account for payment of electricity expenses of the Mega Market.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the City School Board ensure that charges to the SEF are limited to those allowed by law and regulations in order that the purposes for which the fund was established will be attained.
observations.managementAction: The City stopped purchasing office equipment, furniture and fixture for the DepEd City Division.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately correct the erroneous computation of the employer’s share in Pag-IBIG premiums to limit the government share to ₱100.00 per employee based on a maximum compensation of ₱5,000.00 set under the IRR of RA No. 9679 and to explore the possibility of recovering from the Pag-IBIG fund the excess premiums paid such as by considering the excess premiums as advance payments.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant corrected the erroneous computation of the employer’s share in Pag-IBIG premiums by limiting the government share to ₱100.00 per employee.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that payments of financial assistance under the Aid in Crisis Situations Program of the DSWD be covered by individual vouchers and supported by the documents required by DSWD MC No. 04, s. 2015 in order to ensure that these are valid charges to government funds.
observations.managementAction: Payments of financial assistance as of June 30, 2019 were covered by financial vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that effective immediately, the City government stop the payment of allowance to members of the City Peace and Order Council for their attendance to sessions. Require the City to grant to members not more than ₱1,000.00 per meeting and not to exceed ₱4,000.00 per month pursuant to Section 3.1 of LBC No. 73.
observations.managementAction: The City will grant to POC members ₱1,000.00 per meeting attended but not to exceed ₱4,000.00 per month pursuant to Section 3.1 of LBC No. 73.
observations.reason: The City granted adjustments and proposed a revised schedule of allowances but full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City hire more personnel to prepare the required documents for implementation of projects so that the purposes of the funded projects will be attained and projects be prioritized or realigned as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The City hired more personnel to prepare required documents for projects. The City prepared its 10-year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan (separate item) and other steps to facilitate project implementation.
observations.reason: Current appropriations for development projects were not utilized except for loan payments only.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City henceforth stop the utilization of GAD funds for projects which are not gender-related and instead ensure proper usage of the fund so that only appropriate programs, projects and activities may be funded.
observations.managementAction: The City acknowledged and will ensure proper usage; however the City did not submit GAD accomplishment report for CY 2019.
observations.reason: Current GAD reporting (accomplishment report) for CY 2019 was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management revise the recipient conditions set forth in the Deed of Undertaking to ensure grantees repay their loans, require the City Agriculturist to regularly monitor and inspect the status of swine in the hands of loan grantees, require the City Accountant to recognize loans receivable based on Deeds of Undertaking and stop the practice of charging purchases directly to expense while recording the same to inventory and expense accounts; implement prescribed NGAS procedures and institutionalize issuance system.
observations.managementAction: The LPRAT through the BUB Focal Person intensified efforts; the City booked Loans Receivable for the Swine Feeds Program granted in 2014 and 2015 amounting to ₱13,074,118.80 and loan collections amounting to ₱2,760,484.60. Monitoring system implemented; demand letters served to concerned grantees who have unpaid balances; some have begun paying.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and voluminous records across barangays prevented full implementation; reconciliation work ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory items in accordance with existing rules and submit the report not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year; require the GSO to prepare and submit SSMIs supported with Requisition and Issue Slips and require the City Accountant and GSO to maintain manual Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards in their respective offices.
observations.managementAction: Inventory count and submission were started; results submitted for CY 2018. The SSMIs were submitted and verified against Requisition and Issue Slips. A computer software for inventory monitoring is 95% done. The City Accountant maintained Supplies Ledger Cards in 2018.
observations.reason: The physical inventory count was temporarily on hold due to unavailability of some Inventory Team members; full completion pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Human Resource Management Officer carefully assess the necessity of hiring job order workers as against filling up available vacant positions and ensure job order work does not duplicate that of existing regular employees.
observations.managementAction: Work accomplishments were attached to the payroll as part of the functions. The City will carefully assess necessity of hiring job orders against filling vacant positions and ensure no duplication.
observations.reason: Job Orders were still hired to perform clerical functions; documentation and full assessment pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management take concrete steps for the immediate recovery of the advance payments made to the contractors in the total amount of ₱525,847.50 which remain unrecouped for more than a year, and to forfeit the performance and surety bonds, and deduct the advance payment from future payments for their ongoing projects.
observations.managementAction: A conference was conducted with the contractor and during the conference it was agreed that the advance payment will be deducted from future advanced payments for their ongoing projects. The City Legal officer will still initiate legal action to recover the advance payments.
observations.reason: Contractor of one project could not be located; follow-up legal action pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor create a Disposal Committee who shall take charge of the disposal of the unserviceable properties and follow existing guidelines (COA Circular No. 92-386), have the General Services Officer prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) using NGAS format so cost and other important information are indicated.
observations.managementAction: Disposal Committee created and assigned to take charge of disposal of unserviceable properties. The GSO is gathering data on other unserviceable vehicles and properties acquired prior to the creation of the Office of the GSO.
observations.reason: Committee has yet to submit complete list of unserviceable properties and appraisal; GSO still gathering data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management set a time frame for the City’s SWM Board to formulate the new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with R.A. 9003 so as to maximize benefits.
observations.managementAction: The City prepared its 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan for CYs 2020 – 2029.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management improve the implementation of Solid Waste Management pursuant to R.A. 9003 through the following: a) adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of wastes to be collected, b) acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at source and point of collection, c) widen the coverage of the city’s garbage collection system, and d) require the SWM Board to strengthen IEC Program.
observations.managementAction: The City scheduled the conduct of Waste Analyzation and Information, Characterization Survey (WACS) and Information, Education and Communication (IEC) in waste segregation preparation. The City is drafting the 10-year plan and acquired a lot for sanitary landfill.
observations.reason: Needs partnership of barangays and technical expertise on segregation; wider coverage and more trucks pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and to avoid repercussions from non-utilization and delayed implementation.
observations.managementAction: The City undertook steps to fast-track BUB implementation and hired more engineers, job orders and contract of service personnel. Monitoring and supervision implemented.
observations.reason: Lack of technical manpower complement delayed some implementations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRM Council, through the CDRRM Officer, review the LDRRMIP and include all aspects (proper fund utilization and identification of PPAs, time table, people, workload and capability) in its preparation to accelerate implementation so that the 70% Mitigation Fund will be fully utilized.
observations.managementAction: The CDRRM reviewed completed PPAs and unimplemented PPAs; the City reverted balances of completed PPAs and unimplemented PPAs amounting to ₱14,498,677.18 under the projects.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower complement to implement all projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. the City Accountant submit the monthly BRS from 1999 to the present otherwise the sanctions provided under COA Circular should be imposed; and b. the City Mayor assign competent personnel to concentrate on the preparation of the BRS.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant submitted monthly BRS from 1999 to the present as of various dates. The audit recommended filing of administrative charges against those responsible for delayed submissions; the City Mayor assigned personnel to prepare BRS.
observations.reason: Voluminous day-to-day transactions prevented immediate reconciliation and administrative actions; some bank statements missing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive require the conduct of inventory count and submission of report to the audit team, reconcile physical inventory with accounting and property records and emphasize accountability with sanctions under Sections 122 and 127 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Physical inventory count was started October 2018; partial inventory report for CY 2018 was submitted to COA. Inventory Team completed partial count and submitted results for CY 2018.
observations.reason: Physical inventory count was temporarily on hold due to unavailability of some Inventory Team members; only partial inventory accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant maintain livestock ledger cards and update monthly using reports from the City Agriculturist; require the City Accountant to reconcile records on work/zoo animals and breeding stocks with those of the City Agriculturist and draw JEVs for births and deaths as appropriate; require the City Agriculturist to prepare immediate reports on births and deaths and monthly inventory reports.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant prepared subsidiary ledger cards for livestock and the GSO maintained property cards in 2018. The Office of the City Accountant started maintaining records and the GSO is maintaining property cards.
observations.reason: Difficulty in obtaining complete data from the City Agriculturist; GSO maintaining property cards while still reconciling.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the immediate implementation of the projects funded by the national government to avoid further delay, revise unit costs where necessary, and closely monitor project sites and contractor capacity to ensure timely implementation.
observations.managementAction: The City prioritized the implementation and hired more engineers and personnel; 90% of one road project implemented though there were delays due to impassable project site.
observations.reason: Project site impediments (impassable road) caused delays in implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly reports together with supporting documents to the auditor within the reglementary periods, and that salaries of responsible officials be suspended until compliance.
observations.managementAction: Monthly reports and trial balances were submitted to the auditor for various quarters (1st Qtr – 7/26/19; 2nd Qtr – 9/18/19; 3rd Qtr – 12/28/19). The City Accountant also worked on required documents and BRS.
observations.reason: Some reports were submitted late; voluminous day-to-day transactions and manpower complement affected timely submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management concretize methods and procedures for phase out and eventual closure of the open dumpsite, operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site, set a time frame for the SWM Board to formulate a new 10-year ISWM Plan incorporating waste segregation at source and establishment of collection systems, and strengthen IEC programs.
observations.managementAction: The City acquired a lot in Barangay Basak (TCT No. 0017002286, area 104,194 sq.m.) and drafted a Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan and is preparing methods and procedures for the phase out. The City prepared the 10-year plan and is working on sanitary landfill development.
observations.reason: Technical expertise and funding to develop sanitary landfill and complete phase-out pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City ensure that those properties which can be insured be included in the GIF and insure same with the GSIS every year and insure public infrastructures as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The City transferred available premiums to the General Fund and insured the Mega Market; other structures assessed for insurance yet to be insured.
observations.reason: Some public infrastructures are yet to be assessed for insurance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Assessor to prepare the SMV pursuant to Section 212 of the Local Government Code and cause the passage of an Ordinance incorporating the proposed SMV as basis for a general revision of real property assessments in adherence to Section 219 and the DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: The City requested assistance; submitted SMV to BLGF Region VII and requested assistance from Department of Agrarian Reform for covered CARP properties; some requests returned for revision.
observations.reason: Requested assistance returned for revision; some properties under CARP needed coordination.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending government funds in connection with entertainment which do not benefit the public and require responsible persons to refund irregular and unnecessary expenditures upon issuance of Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: No expenses of this nature were incurred in 2019. Previous irregular expenditures were subject to Notice of Disallowance and collection actions.
observations.reason: The incurred amount was not yet fully refunded at time of report; some actions in process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CDRRMO submit the required documents for negotiated procurement, account for distribution of relief goods with distribution lists and acknowledgements, submit explanations for inconsistencies between dates of distribution and inspection, and that the City Accountant ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Some vouchers were retired in 2017; then CDRRMO requested assistance and some vouchers could not be located. The new CDRRMO Officer was not connected with transactions and thus could not be privy to prior actions.
observations.reason: Some vouchers could not be located; documents missing and transactions occurred under previous personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review all balances dormant in Trust Fund books, identify excess balances for transfer to source agencies or transfer to General Fund where appropriate, prepare necessary journal entries to close completed projects and return excess to source agencies, and properly transfer completed projects to General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant transferred cost of completed projects amounting to ₱51,099,175.74 to the General Fund on 10/18/2018 and prepared necessary journal entries to close projects. The accountant returned unutilized balances to respective agencies for some items.
observations.reason: Voluminous day-to-day transactions prevented immediate reconciliation of all dormant balances; manpower constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management still enforce settlement of cash advances by issuing final demand letters to accountable officers and employees and impose sanctions including withholding of salaries where necessary.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent and efforts made to collect. Some concerned grantees have begun paying; many grantees not longer connected with the City or deceased.
observations.reason: Most remaining grantees are no longer connected with the City Government or deceased; some repayments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all officials and employees to refund the overpayment or recover through reduction of future PEI and to henceforth strictly observe limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: The City collected P2,721,720.90 as of June 30, 2019; further collections ongoing. Thereafter, the City to observe strict limitations.
observations.reason: Some recipients are no longer connected with the City; collection efforts continue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and investigate the difference by tracing the erroneous journal entries and prepare necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts and regularly reconcile these reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: The City started tracing and reconciling the journal entries but voluminous transactions impeded immediate full reconciliation.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel privy to the voluminous transactions and records booked in 2014 and prior years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City immediately transfer its deposits with PNB amounting to ₱110,814,414.16 to the LBP-Guihulngan Branch and prepare monthly bank reconciliation statements for those accounts.
observations.managementAction: Only two deposit accounts remained with PNB; the City prepared and submitted BRS for submitted accounts, but preparation of BRS for remaining accounts was delayed due to missing bank statements and subsidiary ledgers.
observations.reason: Difficulty preparing BRS for remaining accounts due to unavailability of bank statements and subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that personnel responsible for processing payments for gasoline and oil which resulted to overpayment of P382,826.67 be required to restitute the amount and that City Accountant ensure payments are approved only for quantities actually delivered.
observations.managementAction: Demand was already issued to the supplier and inspection of items delivered required to ensure propriety of payments. Some refunds being pursued.
observations.reason: Refund was not yet made by the supplier at time of report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that completed projects be transferred to the General Fund by identifying properties, plant and equipment and transfer cost to General Fund, prepare PPELC and recognize depreciation, and require LGU to maintain properly accomplished PPELC.
observations.managementAction: Cost of completed projects amounting to ₱51,099,175.74 was transferred to the General Fund on 10/18/2018 and corresponding PPELC entries maintained. The City prepared necessary entries and maintained PPELC.
observations.reason: Some PPEs were transferred and recognized; complete PPELC maintenance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the position be approved and filled up by employing an educationally-qualified and properly motivated person to discharge functions of a GSO in pursuance of the Local Government Code.
observations.managementAction: The creation of the Government Services Officer was approved on September 14, 2016 and City Administrator Gian Carlo A. Mijares was designated as GSO; a full-time GSO still to be hired.
observations.reason: Full-time GSO has yet to be hired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management follow COA Training Handbook and dispose of unserviceable properties through Disposal Committee and proper procedures for realization of salvage value.
observations.managementAction: Disposal Committee created and taking charge of disposal; GSO gathering data on other unserviceable properties acquired prior to creation of GSO.
observations.reason: Committee has yet to submit complete list and carry out disposal; data gathering ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management thoroughly review validity of outstanding payables, remit to individuals/agencies those payables found valid and request for reversion of liabilities with no valid claim, and strictly comply with regulations in recognition and recording of liabilities.
observations.managementAction: The City prepared aging schedules and reviewed accounts. Some liabilities were remitted where claimants were identified; efforts to revert undocumented liabilities ongoing.
observations.reason: Unavailability of records and manpower constrained full resolution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer’s Office, Accounting Office and the City Assessor exert extra effort to maintain subsidiary ledgers for all land accounts, prepare and maintain proper subsidiary records of real properties, and prioritize filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the City so that transfer certificates of title can be issued.
observations.managementAction: The City prepared and maintained subsidiary ledgers for land accounts and coordinated with City Assessor to prioritize titling of properties acquired by the City.
observations.reason: Some records prepared but complete titling and subsidiary records remain in progress; lack of complete documents to support all parcels.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of disbursements, and henceforth, observe strictly the policies and procedures under the revised IRR of the procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR None. Procurement from PS-DBM is not viable as PS-DBM is located in Cebu City and procurement will entail additional costs for freight, courier, travelling and fuel.
observations.reason: PS-DBM office is located in Cebu City. Procurement of common-use supplies from Procurement Service would entail additional costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management find ways and means to utilize the slaughterhouse to serve its intended purpose that would ultimately redound to the benefit of all constituents of the city. Ensure its proper maintenance to prevent deterioration and to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary and high cost of repair.
observations.managementAction: CY 2009 AAR The slaughterhouse is the subject of an investigation being conducted by the Ombudsman. Improvements and utilization are being considered upon resolution of the case.
observations.reason: The matter is the subject of investigation by the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the LDRRM Council
pass a resolution reverting the CYs 2010 to 2013 LDRRMF unspent balances
to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, for adoption by the
Sangguniang Bayan thru the Local Development Council. We further
recommended that the members of the Local Finance Committee prepare the
necessary transfer of funds and accounting reports to facilitate the said
reversion and certify to the availability of said funds for subsequent
reappropriation for other social services.

We recommended and Management agreed that the rules and
regulations on the use of government vehicles be strictly implemented by
requiring, for each and every trip, a trip ticket which contains all the required
information before it is approved, by limiting vehicle use to official purposes
only, by assigning personnel to monitor the reasonableness of fuel
consumption, by ensuring that the odometers of all vehicles are working
properly, and by requiring passengers to sign the certification as to official use.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City consolidate
its fuel requisitions and procure its needs through public bidding as required
under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR in order that it can avail of the most
advantageous prices and of bulk discounts, if any, and also be shielded from
the constant increase in the prices of fuel.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant
take up payments for electricity consumption of occupants of the Mega
Market as trust liabilities in the special account Market Operations, and to
charge to this account the payments to the electric service provider, in order
to fairly present the assets, liabilities and cumulative results of operations
thereof in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that henceforth, the City
School Board ensure that charges to the SEF are limited to those allowed by
law and regulations in order that the purposes for which the fund was
established will be attained.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City government
immediately correct the erroneous computation of the employer’s share in
Pag-IBIG premiums by limiting government share to ₱100.00 per employee
based on a maximum compensation of ₱5,000.00 set under the IRR of RA No.
9679 and to explore the possibility of recovering from the Pag-IBIG fund the
excess premiums paid such as by considering the excess premiums as advance
payments.

We recommended and Management agreed that payments of financial
assistance under the Aid in Crisis Situations Program of the DSWD be covered
by individual vouchers and supported by the documents required under
DSWD MC No. 04, s. 2015 in order to ensure that these are valid charges to
government funds.

We recommended and Management agreed that effective immediately,
the City government stop the payment of allowance to members of the City
Peace and Order Council for their attendance to sessions.

We recommended and Management agreed to hire more engineering
personnel to prepare the required documents for the implementation of
projects. We further recommended that the City Development Council review
the feasibility and relevance of development projects funded by prior years’
20% Development Fund which have remained unimplemented to date in order
that these may be realigned to other currently-needed projects or else be given
priority of implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed that the City henceforth
stop the utilization of GAD funds for projects which are not gender-related
and instead ensure proper usage of the fund in order that only the appropriate
programs, projects and activities may be funded thereby.

We recommended that management: a. Revise the conditions set forth in the Deed of Undertaking to ensure that the grantees repay their loans whether or not the animals survived and to impose penalties or legal remedies for non-payment; b. Require the City Agriculturist to regularly monitor and inspect the status of swine in the hands of loan grantees, to encourage grantees to repay their loans and to reconcile her records with the accounting records; c. Require the City Accountant to recognize loan receivables for loans granted on the basis of the Deeds of Undertaking signed by the recipients; and d. Stop the practice of directly charging the purchases of swine feeds to expense and simultaneously recording the same to the related inventory and expense accounts upon purchase and instead implement the prescribed procedures for the purchase and utilization of supplies and materials pursuant to the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR revised to make Undertaking was revised to make the recipient liable for repayment regardless of outcome, without prejudice to the power of the Sangguniang Panlungsod to grant amnesty or reprieve when it is justified. Demand letters were served on recipients who have unpaid obligations. Monitoring system is already in place. Booked Loans Receivable for the Swine Feeds Program granted in 2014 and 2015 amounting to ₱2,760,484.60 in January 2018. Transferred Ms. Arlene Aba, Contract of Service, in September 2018 from the Office of the City Accountant to the Office of the City Agriculturist to assist in the compliance of the recommendation s. Institutionalized the use of the RIS in the issuance of supplies and materials.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to reconcile voluminous records from the barangays of the city.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the GSO and the Committee to conduct the physical count of inventory items in accordance with existing rules and regulations and to submit the required report thereon to the auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively; b. Require the GSO to prepare and submit to the City Accountant the SSMI for all inventory items issued during the year for the preparation of the necessary Summaries of adjusting entries to correct the inventory accounts; c. Require the GSO to henceforth, prepare the SSMI supported with the related inventory Requisition Issue Slips and submit the same to the City Accountant on a weekly basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher in compliance with Sections 51 and 121 of NGAS Manual Volume I and Sections 41 and 43 of NGAS Manual Volume II; and d. Require the City Accountant and GSO to henceforth maintain the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards in their respective offices.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR Inventory count was still to be conducted by the office and the SSMIs and the corresponding utilization report. The office was able to submit an inventory report as of December 31, 2018 to the auditor. The GSO has prepared Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued as of June 26, 2018 to the COA. A computer software for the GSO is 95% done to fully adapt to monitoring and filing needs of the office. The GSO started maintaining Supplies Ledger Cards in 2018.
observations.reason: The SSMIs submitted are verified against the corresponding Requisition and Issue Slips.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the City Assessor and Treasurer to update the real data/information on Real Property Tax Assessment Roll and furnish the Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers due and amounts provided by the City Treasurer; b. Require the City Accountant to recognize receivables from RPT/SET at the beginning of each year on the basis of the Certified List of Taxpayers provided by the City Treasurer; c. Require the City Accountant to record collections of RPT/SET using the following entries: 1) Upon Collection Dr: Cash in Treasury Cr: Real Property Tax Receivable 2) Upon Distribution of Collection Dr: Deferred RPT Income Cr: RPT Cr: Due to LGUs; d. Require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to conduct reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of Receivables from the Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes so that necessary adjustments in the books of accounts can be effected for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and e. Require the City Treasurer to conduct information campaign to all real property owners within the LGU and notify them of the risk of losing their properties in case of failure to pay their taxes. Otherwise, civil remedies should be employed.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR The City Assessor and Integrated Taxation Management System (iTax), a program that aids the LGU to address issues in tax collection, update data/information on real property tax assessment roll. Receivables from RPT/SET was set up using the recommended journal entries at the beginning of CY 2018 based on the certified list of taxpayers provided by the City Treasurer. Receivables from RPT/SET were set up using the recommended collections entries. Recommended entries were likewise used in booking collections. The City Treasurer conducted information dissemination on remedies available to delinquent tax payers: penalties for non-payment were imposed and request for payment made.
observations.reason: Receivables from RPT/SET was set up using the recommended journal entries. Some reconciliations remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Human Resource Management Officer carefully assess the necessity of hiring job order workers as against filling up available vacant plantilla positions, and ensure that the work to be done by these job order workers are not duplicate those of existing regular employees.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR Work Accomplishment requirements were attached to the payroll as part of the administrative documentary functions. Job Orders were carefully assessed against available plantilla positions. Job Orders were implemented with accompanying Accomplishment Requirements.
observations.reason: Not implemented to fill up vacant positions; some Job Orders were still hired to perform clerical functions. Records attached to payrolls as part of administrative documentary functions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor take concrete steps for the immediate recovery of the advance payments made to the contractors in the amount of ₱525,847.50 which remain unrecouped for more than a year already.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR A conference was conducted by the city government with the third representative of the contractor of the involved projects.
