It was agreed during the conference that said advance payment will be deducted from future payments. The City Legal Office will still initiate legal action to recover the amount advanced.
observations.reason: The city was not able to locate contractor of the project. It was only recently that the Engineering Office was informed that contractor had passed away. The city will still initiate legal action to recover the advanced amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management maintain the Petty Cash Fund under the imprest system as follows: a. The Disbursing Officer draw an initial cash advance to establish the Petty Cash Fund and at the beginning of the year, to be replenished as soon as disbursements reach 75%, to be fully liquidated at the end of the year. The amount shall be sufficient for the non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses of the LGU for one month. b. Only petty expenses shall be paid from said cash advance through the use of the Petty Cash Voucher (PCV). Regular expenses such as rentals, subscriptions, utilities shall not be paid from the PCF.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR Petty Cash Fund was set up in May 2018 and maintained under imprest system. Used the Petty Cash Fund to pay non-recurring expenses covered by Petty Cash Vouchers amounting to ₱1,000.00 and below.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor create a Disposal Committee who shall take charge the disposal of the unserviceable properties, following existing guidelines such as COA Circular No. 92-386 (Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Office of the Local Government); and that the General Services Officer prepares the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) using the NGAS-prescribed format so that important information such as the cost thereof will be indicated.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 AAR Created Disposal Committee to take charge the disposal of the unserviceable properties. The General Services Office prepared IIRUP. Some items were disposed through auction. The GSO is still gathering data on other unserviceable vehicles and equipment.
observations.reason: Disposal committee has yet to submit the complete list of unserviceable properties, equipment and waste materials to COA for assessment/appraisal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local chief executive instruct the City’s SWM Board to formulate the new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in accordance with the provisions of R.A. 9003 in order to maximize the pervasive benefits considering the length of time that has lapsed since the expiration of the city’s 2002 ISWM Plan.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The Sangguniang Panlungsod created the City Environment and Natural Resources Council and Institutionalized the Use of the 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan. The Office of the City Environment Coordinator and City Development Coordinator facilitate the drafting of the 10-year plan. The City acquired property for the sanitary landfill in 2017. Submission to DENR-EMB is ongoing.
observations.reason: Partially implemented due to lack of technical expertise to assist the SWM Board in formulating the 10-Year ISWM Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of wastes to be collected; b) Acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at source and point of collection; c) Widen the coverage of the city’s garbage collection system; and d) Require the SWM Board to strengthen their Information, Education and Communication (IEC) Program to increase the awareness of the constituents on waste management.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The City scheduled the conduct of Waste Analyzation and Characterization Survey (WACS) in preparation for implementation of the waste segregation program. The city acquired more garbage trucks and increased coverage of collection in barangays.
observations.reason: Needs the partnership of the city and the barangays on the Information, Education and Communication program in waste segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the implementation of projects funded by the 20% Development Fund and that the CPDC and CDC adequately and thoroughly plan all infrastructure projects prior to their inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure that only those which can be implemented within two years are included. We further recommended that management provide the City Engineering Office with enough personnel to ensure immediate implementation and completion of projects within the specified timeframe.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Five engineers were hired during 2016 and 2017 to fast-track the implementation of the projects. The City Planning and Development Coordinator and the City Development Council, through the City Mayor, monitor, review, update and prioritize the projects. Status of the 16 development works: 1-Completed, 4-On-going, 5-On bidding process, 3-Finalization of DED and POW, 3-Encountered RROW problem.
observations.reason: Lack of technical manpower to do the job, like making detailed design and program of works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the BUB Focal Person, intensify efforts to fast-track the implementation of the BUB program for the LGU to achieve its target of reducing poverty and likewise avoid any repercussions that may result from its non-utilization of BUB Funds as well as delayed implementation of related programs/projects.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR The City intensified efforts to fast-track BUB implementation. Assigned technical personnel to focus on BUB projects and to closely monitor progress thereon. Some projects were implemented and monitoring was enhanced.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower complement to implement the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LPRAT, through the CDRRM Officer, review the completed PPAs, include all aspects of related matters (proper utilization and identification of PPAs, time table, people, workload and others) in its preparation in order to accelerate the implementation of the projects, programs and activities within the year so that the 70% Mitigation Fund of the LDRRMF will be fully utilized in accordance with the purpose.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR The City DRRM Office, through the CDRRM Officer, implemented completed PPAs amounting to ₱14,498,677.18 under the continuing appropriations. The LDRRM Council reviewed PPAs and included necessary components. Some PPAs were reprogrammed for implementation.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to implement the project.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that: a. The City Accountant submit to the auditor the bank reconciliation statements (BRS) monthly from 1999 to present, otherwise the charges against those responsible for the delayed submission shall be imposed; and b. The City Mayor assign competent and knowledgeable administrative personnel to concentrate and prepare the BRS.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The Office of the City Accountant was able to submit 1,832 monthly bank reconciliation statements for the period 1999 to 2017 covering 23 bank accounts maintained by the City government. As of December 31, 2018, the variance between the balances of cash in bank per books and per bank amounted to P1,654,000.57. The City Mayor assigned competent accounting personnel to focus on preparing the BRS. Some accounts could not be reproduced due to the lapse of the 10-year retention period of records.
observations.reason: Massive volume of transactions for more than 20 years. Some accounts did not have BRS as far as 1992. Lack of competent personnel; some bank statements and subsidiary ledgers were unavailable and could not be reproduced due to lapse of retention period.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the conduct of the annual physical inventory count of properties and that the Inventory Committee plan and strategize the conduct of the physical count of properties every year, giving emphasis to accountability for such properties and the sanctions provided under Sections 122 and 127 of P.D. 1445.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Physical inventory count was started last October 2018 but the Inventory Team failed to complete the count. Only partial inventory count was conducted and submitted to the audit team for CY 2018. Some members of the Inventory Team were unavailable. The GSO is gathering data on properties and PPE.
observations.reason: Physical inventory count was temporarily on hold because unavailability of some members of the Inventory Team. Voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a) Require the City Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to update these cards and to maintain property cards for the livestock. b) Require the City Accountant to reconcile the records on work/zoo animals and breeding stocks with those maintained by the City Agriculturist, and draw the JEV for the reported birth of offspring, or drop from the record in case of death, transfer or sale of work animals/breeding stocks that are traceable and are part of the originally recorded work animals/breeding stocks; and c) Henceforth, require the City Agriculturist to prepare a report within two days from the birth of animals. In the case of death of offspring, the City Agriculturist shall likewise submit a report within two days stating the value of the animal, causes/reasons of death and other necessary details. Likewise, a Monthly Report of Inventory shall be prepared for all animals within 10 days after the end of the month, copy furnished the GSO and the City Accountant.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The City Accountant has prepared Livestock Ledger Cards while the GSO is maintaining livestock ledger cards and property cards for the livestock. The Office of the City Accountant reconciled records on animals with reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and the livestock ledger cards were prepared. Monthly reports are being required.
observations.reason: Difficulty in obtaining complete data from the Office of the City Agriculturist.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The Sangguniang Panlungsod institute internal rules to regulate the purchase and assignment of cellular phones and the charging of mobile telephone expenses, and to consider limits as to rank, cost of mobile cellphone, post-paid plans and number of units; b. The cellular phones that come from the postpaid subscriptions be recorded in the books as government assets and covered by Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) upon issuance to the end-users; c. City officials up to department head level who were issued or holding multiple cellphones either from requisition or postpaid plan return the excess and keep only one unit while employees below department head level who are not entitled to the privilege be required to return their units to the General Services Office for disposal through auction sale; and d. Management disapprove the charging to LGU funds unnecessary and unconscionable mobile telephone expenses such as monthly amortization/one-time charge for cellphone upgrade, additional subscription on top of existing ones, Gadget Care and Share Load, and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure to immediately refund the amount disbursed upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance which shall be issued by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR The Sangguniang Panlungsod passed on November 28, 2017 Ordinance No. 2017-25 – Prescribing the Guidelines in the Use or Availment of Government-Issued Cellular Phones, Cellular Phones, Prepaid Load and Post-Paid Mobile and/or Broadband Plans. The cost of the units for Communication Equipment are now booked as Government Equipment. The City Accountant is requiring the submission or attachment of Requisition and Issue Slips to support the charging of purchases to the related inventory and expense accounts.
observations.reason: The PS-DBM office is located in Cebu City. Procurement of common-use supplies from Procurement Service would entail additional costs e.g. freight, courier, travelling expenses, fuel and lubricants for vehicle.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management prioritize the immediate implementation of the projects funded by the national government in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the implementation of the identified programs, projects and activities (PPAs).
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Projects were implemented; some were delayed due to project site being impassable and other technical issues. The city recovered from delays and continued implementation.
observations.reason: The project site has impassable road that caused the delay of the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Secure from concerned suppliers/service providers the required warranty certificates on repairs and maintenance of vehicles and equipment; b. Require the GSO to maintain records of the history of repairs on equipment and vehicles and submit the same to the auditor when repairs and maintenance is undertaken; and c. Henceforth, ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached to the DVs.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 AAR Warranty certificates for repairs and maintenance of vehicles and equipment are being secured. The GSO is maintaining records of the history of repairs on equipment and vehicles and these are submitted to the auditor as required. Procedures for documentation are in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant prepare and submit the monthly reports together with the duplicate official receipts, paid vouchers and their supporting schedules and documents, bank reconciliation statements and trial balances to the auditor within the reglementary period; otherwise cause the suspension of salaries of responsible officials who fail to submit.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR The City started tracing the journal entries but found difficulty due to the volume of transactions. The City prepared and submitted quarterly reports as follows: 1st Qtr. – 7/20/18; 2nd Qtr. – 10/25/18; 3rd Qtr. – 12/18/18; 4th Qtr. – 3/22/19. The city adopted eNGAS and eBudgeting systems of the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Voluminous transactions and lack of manpower complement. Some reports were submitted late.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Concretize methods and procedures for the phase out and eventual closure and rehabilitation of the existing open dumpsite within a specific time frame; b) Endeavor to operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid wastes; c) Set a time frame for the City Solid Waste Management Board to formulate a new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan incorporating therewith specific guidelines on waste segregation at source and the establishment of collection systems for segregated solid waste; and d) Require the SWM Board to strengthen their Information, Education and Communication (IEC) Program to increase the awareness of the constituents on waste management.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR The City prepared the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan and acquired a lot in 2017 to be developed as the City’s sanitary landfill. The plan is being prepared and will be submitted to DENR-EMB.
observations.reason: Needs the plan submission to DENR-EMB and further IEC strengthening.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Fund and insure the city’s properties with the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR All of the City’s vehicles have been insured. The city insured Mega Market with the GSIS. The city assessed other infrastructures for insurance.
observations.reason: Assets and infrastructures needs assessment for premium payment; some infrastructures yet to be insured.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Assessor prepare and conduct a general revision of real property assessments in adherence to Section 219 of the Local Government Code and DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2010-001 by preparing an Ordinance incorporating therein the proposed SMV as basis for the conduct of a general revision of real property assessments in adherence with the law.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR Requested assistance from Department of Agrarian Reform for CARP beneficiaries. Prepared draft ordinance and submitted for revision.
observations.reason: Submitted to BLGF Region VII but returned for revision.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending government funds on activities that do not benefit the City and instead require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure to immediately refund the amount disbursed upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance to be issued by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: CY 2015 AAR No expenses of this nature were incurred in 2018. Written explanation on the matter was submitted in January to June CY 2014. Refunds were made where necessary.
observations.reason: The amount incurred was not yet refunded. Some persons responsible in 2014 are no longer connected with the City Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Submit the required documents to determine the validity of the transactions; b) Require the CDRRMO and City Accountant to account for the distribution of said food items and relief goods by submitting the distribution list of beneficiaries with addresses and their acknowledgements; c) Submit a written explanation with regard to the inconsistencies between the date of distribution and the date of acceptance and inspection of subject emergency items; and d) Ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents to establish the validity of claims for payment.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Retrieved some of the vouchers to determine the lacking documents. The new CDRRMO was not connected with the City Government at the time the purchases were made and could not be privy to the transaction. The City submitted the distribution lists and some beneficiaries’ acknowledgments.
observations.reason: Some vouchers could not yet be located. The new CDRRMO at the time of audit was not connected to the City Government during the purchases.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management review all balances in the Trust Fund books for two (2) years to more than ten (10) years and identify excess balances for return to source agencies. Transfer completed projects to General Fund books and prepare the necessary journal entries to close completed projects. Return excess to source agencies.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Transferred cost of completed projects amounting to P51,099,175.74 to the General Fund on 10/18/2018. Same PPEs were booked in the General Fund on 10/24/2018. Liquidated funds were returned to the respective source agencies: Dept of Agriculture – ₱200,000.00; Dept of Labor and Employment – ₱15,000.00; DSWD – ₱2,670.00; DPWH – ₱251,275.00; BFAR – ₱860.00.
observations.reason: Voluminous day-to-day transactions prevented immediate review. Lack of manpower complement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management still enforce settlement of cash advances by issuing final demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees and to impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of those who fail to liquidate as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Sent demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees, after which imposed the sanction of withholding salaries. As a result of intensive collection efforts, the balance of Unliquidated Cash Advances as of December 31, 2014 was reduced to P632,633.47 or by 96.96% as of December 31, 2018.
observations.reason: Most of the remaining grantees are no longer connected with the City Government. Some recipients are deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all city officials and employees to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive and henceforth, to observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Collected the amount of December 31, 2018 or a reduction of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 of 30.67%. The City is now enforcing proper PEI limits.
observations.reason: Some recipients are no longer connected with the City Government; some are deceased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Stop the practice of directly charging the related purchases of inventory items to expense or related asset account as well as proper charging of purchases to the related inventory and expense accounts and instead implement the prescribed procedures for the purchase and utilization of supplies and materials pursuant to Sections 51, 114, 116 and 121 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; b. Require end-user departments to prepare Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) for utilization for submission to the GSO on a weekly basis; c. Require the GSO to consolidate on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) all RIS and submit the same to the accountant weekly; and d. Require the accountant to record to the expense accounts the supplies consumed on the basis of the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with GSO records.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR The City is now requiring submission or attachment of Requisition and Issue Slips to support the charging of purchases to the related inventory and expense accounts. The GSO consolidated SSMI and submitted to the Accountant. The City Accountant is recording expenses based on SSMI. Procedures were institutionalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant review and investigate the difference by tracing the erroneous journal entries, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts in the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books and to henceforth regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Started tracing the journal entries but found difficulties due to the volume of transactions booked in 2014 and years prior. Prepared reconciliations and necessary adjustments were being made.
observations.reason: Voluminous transactions. Lack of personnel privy to the transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Management Fund to the Office of the Auditor before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the review and monitoring of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: CY 2014 AAR Submitted the monthly reports on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Management Fund to the Office of the Auditor as required under Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate the review and monitoring of disbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Immediately transfer the city’s deposits in PNB to LBP-Guihulngan Branch; and b) prepare monthly BRS for all accounts and ensure timely submission of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR Only 2 deposit accounts remained with PNB with a total bank balance of ₱1,236,492.29 as of 12/31/2018, or 98.88% reduction in deposit. The City is preparing BRS for these accounts. The City is working to transfer remaining deposits to LBP-Guihulngan.
observations.reason: Difficulty in preparation of monthly BRS due to unavailability of bank statements and subsidiary ledgers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Stop the hiring of job orders and charging of their wages to appropriations of the city for legislative research which act is illegal and punishable by law; b) Submit a written explanation establishing the necessity of hiring 466 JOs for the period January to June CY 2013 coinciding with the election ban period; c) Furnish the audit team with the output of the legislative research project of the SP and Mayor’s Office such as newly approved/drafts of ordinances and resolutions based from the researches; and d) Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 336 of the Local Government Code and Article VI, Section 29 of the Philippine Constitution which require that funds shall be used only for the specific purpose for which it was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR A written explanation was submitted. Outputs of legislative research were attached to payrolls. The City assured the audit team that due diligence is observed in charging of expenditures to the respective appropriations.
observations.reason: Lack of documents to support the procurement and outputs. Some Job Order outputs were submitted with payrolls.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Prior to any repair and requisition of spare parts, pre-repair inspection must be conducted by the Maintenance and Engineering department and documented in a Pre-Repair Evaluation Report; b) Pre-repair inspection conducted by authorized personnel shall be documented in a Pre-Repair Inspection Report; c) Requisitions must be prepared by the head of office or department and Waste Material Reports signed by the person accountable thereof; and d) All deliveries of items purchased must first be received by the GSO.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR Procedures are now in place. The City requires pre-repair inspection reports and documentation prior to repair. Requisitions are prepared and GSO receives deliveries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The city’s fuel consumption be properly controlled and regulated through the issuance of properly accomplished and serially numbered driver’s trip tickets; b. Drivers be required to prepare Monthly Reports of Official Travel summarizing the trip tickets of their respective vehicles, to be approved by the Administrative Officer; c. The General Services Office be required to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification purposes; d. The Accountant should process claims for payment of gasoline and oil only if completely supported with complete documentation; and e. Acceptance and Inspection Report must be signed by the General Services Officer/Supply Officer as the officer receiving the delivery.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR The City Administrator is now requiring the drivers to accomplish trip tickets. Monthly reports are being prepared by the drivers and consolidated by GSO. The Accountant processes claims only with complete documentation. Acceptance and Inspection Reports are signed by the GSO.
observations.reason: Most of the trips are emergency in nature and the drivers could not accomplish all details. However, the City now requires trip tickets to be accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Require the personnel directly responsible for the processing of payments for gasoline and oil to restitute the amount; and b. Henceforth, the City Accountant and accounting personnel in charge of processing payments for fuel be instructed to approve payment for just the quantity actually delivered.
observations.managementAction: CY 2013 AAR Demand was already issued to the concerned suppliers. The City required inspection of items delivered to ensure propriety of payments. Some refunds were made.
observations.reason: Refund was not yet made by the supplier for certain items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Conduct a physical inventory of all properties involved to determine the existence and condition of these assets. b. Require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of the general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of the NGAS. c. Require the LGU to maintain a properly accomplished/updated Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and the auditor.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 AAR Transferred cost of completed projects amounting to ₱51,099,175.74 to the General Fund on 10/18/2018. Same PPEs were booked in the General Fund on 10/24/2018. Prepared inventory and conducted physical count for some properties and transferred to the General Fund.
observations.reason: Physical count was started but voluminous transactions and records complexity delayed completion. GSO is still gathering data. Voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately employ and/or appoint an educationally-qualified and properly motivated person to discharge the functions of a GSO.
observations.managementAction: CY 2012 AAR The creation of the position General Services Officer was approved on September 14, 2016 and City Administrator Gian Carlo A. Mijares has been designated as General Services Officer in pursuance of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: The City still has to hire a full-time GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the department heads concerned to identify the assets and liabilities amounting to ₱39,058,925.59 and ₱4,626,720.56, respectively, which are still not properly identified and prepare the necessary adjusting entries so that the accounts can be fairly presented and so that generally accepted accounting principles on recording as well as procedures on property and supply management can be complied with.
observations.managementAction: CY 2011 AAR Assets and liabilities are being investigated. Schedules were prepared showing identified accounts amounting to ₱39,058,925.59 and liabilities ₱4,626,720.56. Adjusting journal entries were prepared for those identified accounts.
observations.reason: Accounts are now supported with schedules.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management consider disposing of its unserviceable properties within the year or the soonest possible time in order to realize additional income, otherwise these unserviceable properties may continue to deteriorate and eventually loses their salvage values. Management is further advised to be guided by Chapter 6 of the COA Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System in the disposal process.
observations.managementAction: CY 2007 AAR Created Disposal Committee to take charge the disposal of the unserviceable properties. The General Services Office prepared necessary IIRUPs and initiated disposal through public auction for some items.
observations.reason: Disposal committee has yet to submit the complete list of unserviceable properties, equipment and waste materials to COA for assessment/appraisal. GSO is still gathering data on other unserviceable vehicles and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management thoroughly review the validity of those long outstanding payable accounts, remit to individuals/agencies concerned like the GSIS, PAG-IBIG, Other LGUs, and those payables found to be valid, and request for reversion back to surplus all those which have remained outstanding in the books for more than two years and for which no administrative or judicial claim has been filed. Also strictly comply with regulations in the recognition and recording of liabilities.
observations.managementAction: CY 2009 AAR Prepared aging schedule of payable accounts. Reviewed the validity of accounts and remitted to concerned agencies such as GSIS, PAG-IBIG and LGUs. Reverted those payable accounts found invalid to surplus and made necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: Some liabilities remained unpaid due to lack of supporting records and voluminous transactions and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Treasurer’s Office, Accounting Office and the City Assessor’s Office reconcile their land records on real properties and maintain proper subsidiary records for all parcels of land owned by the City Government. Also, the City Assessor to exert extra effort to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the city government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the City of Guihulngan can be issued to establish the city’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: CY 2007 AAR The City prepared and maintained subsidiary ledgers for land. The City Assessor and Treasurer coordinated to reconcile land records and maintain proper subsidiary ledgers. The City Assessor prioritized filing of applications for titling of city-owned real properties.
observations.reason: Unavailability of complete documents to support the booking of land. Extra effort is ongoing to secure transfer certificates of title.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of disbursements, and henceforth, observe strictly the policies and procedures under the revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies; b. Require the CDRRMO and City Accountant to account for the distribution of the emergency items by submitting to the audit team the distribution list of beneficiaries (with addresses) affected by the typhoon and their acknowledgement of the goods received, as well as the description and quantity of items given per bag which should reconcile with the total goods purchased; c. Submit a written explanation with regard to the inconsistencies between the date of distribution and the date of acceptance and inspection of said emergency items; and d. Require the City Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: No action taken at the time of the report. The CDRRMO indicated that negotiated procurement was used due to urgency. Subsequent documentation efforts were undertaken but original procurement supporting documents were not all available.
observations.reason: Difficulty in identifying the cause of discrepancy between the date of distribution and acceptance due to unavailability of records. Some required documents were not available; original procurement documents lacking.

observations.recommendation: Require the City to submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of disbursements, and to henceforth observe strictly the policies and procedures under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction: No action yet recorded in the report.
observations.reason: Difficulty in procuring some supplies from PS-DBM; arrangements being reviewed.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to review and investigate the difference by tracing the erroneous journal entries, prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts and henceforth regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action recorded. Review of transactions is ongoing; some reconciliations are being undertaken.
observations.reason: Difficulty in identifying the cause of discrepancy due to volume of transactions and unavailability of some records.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management find ways and means to utilize the slaughterhouse to serve its intended purpose and ensure its proper maintenance to prevent deterioration and vandalism and avoid the incurrence of unnecessary and high cost of repair.
observations.managementAction: The slaughterhouse is the subject of an investigation being undertaken by the Ombudsman. Pending resolution, the City is evaluating options for utilization.
observations.reason: Under investigation by the Ombudsman.

observations.recommendation:
MANAGEMENT COMMENT

During the exit conference, management concurred with the audit
recommendations and committed to pursue remedies to ensure payment from loan
grantees. The City Agriculturist also promised to reconcile her records with the
accounting records.

RECOMMENDATIONS

We recommended that management:

a. Revise the conditions set forth in the Deed of Undertaking to ensure that
the grantees repay their loans whether or not the animals survived and to
impose penalties or legal remedies for non-payment;

b. Require the City Agriculturist to regularly monitor and inspect the status
of swine in the hands of loan grantees, to encourage grantees to repay
their loans and to reconcile her records with the accounting records;

c. Require the City Accountant to recognize loan receivables for loans
granted on the basis of the Deeds of Undertaking signed by the recipients;
and

d. Stop the practice of directly charging the purchases of swine feeds to
expense and simultaneously recording the same to the related inventory
and expense accounts upon purchase and instead implement the
prescribed procedures for the purchase and utilization of supplies and
materials pursuant to the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

Management agreed with the audit recommendations and committed to
comply accordingly.

RECOMMENDATION

We recommended that management:

a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of
inventory items in accordance with existing rules and regulations and to
submit the required report thereon to the Auditor concerned not later
than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semester, respectively;

b. Require the GSO to prepare and submit to the City Accountant the SSMI
for all inventory items issued during the year for the preparation of the
necessary adjusting entries to correct the inventory accounts;

c. Require the GSO to henceforth, prepare the SSMI supported with the
related Requisition Issue Slips and submit the same to the City
Accountant on a weekly basis for the preparation of the Journal Entry
Voucher in compliance with Sections 51 and 121 of NGAS Manual
Volume I and Sections 41 and 43 of NGAS Manual Volume II; and

d. Require the City Accountant and GSO to henceforth maintain the
Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards in their respective offices.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

During the exit conference, the City Treasurer concurred with the audit
recommendations and informed the audit team that for CY 2018, she has already
furnished the City Accountant the certified list of taxpayers containing taxes due and
collectible.

Also, the City Accountant has already applied the recognition of receivables
from RPT/SET at the beginning of 2018.

RECOMMENDATION

We recommended that management:

a. Require the City Assessor and Treasurer to update the data/information
on Real Property Tax Assessment Roll and furnish the City Accountant
with the certified list of taxpayers containing taxes due and collectible as
basis for recording the Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables
at the beginning of the year as required in Section 20 of the Manual on
the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs;

b. Require the City Accountant to recognize receivables from RPT/SET at
the beginning of each year on the basis of the Certified List of Taxpayers
and Amounts Due;

c. Require the City Accountant to record collections of RPT/SET using the
following entries:

1) Upon Collection
Cash in Treasury x x
Real Property Tax Receivable x x

2) Upon Distribution of Collection
Deferred RPT Income x x
RPT x x
Due to LGUs x x

d. Require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to conduct
reconciliation of records to establish a realistic balance of Receivables
from the Real Property Taxes and Special Education Taxes so that
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts can be effected for fair
presentation of balances in the financial statements; and

e. Require the City Treasurer to conduct information campaign to all real
property owners within the LGU and notify them of the risk of losing
their properties in case of failure to pay their taxes. Otherwise, civil
remedies provided in cases of non-payment on the part of the taxpayers
despite warnings, should be employed.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

During the exit conference, the HRMO committed to comply with the
audit recommendation and promised to fill up available vacant positions in 2018.

RECOMMENDATION

We recommended that the City Mayor and the City Human Resource
Management Officer carefully assess the necessity of hiring job order
workers as against filling up available vacant positions, and ensure that the
work to be done by these workers are not available in the LGU nor duplicate
those of existing regular employees.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

During the exit conference, the City Legal Officer promised to investigate the
matter and pursue the recovery of the advance payments made to the contractors.

RECOMMENDATION

We recommended that the City Legal Office take concrete steps for the
immediate recovery of the advance payments made to the contractors in the total
amount of ₱525,847.50 which remain unrecouped for more than a year already.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

Management agreed with the audit recommendations and committed to comply
accordingly.

RECOMMENDATIONS

We recommended that management maintain the Petty Cash Fund under the
imprest system as follows:

a. The Disbursing Officer draw an initial cash advance to establish the Petty
Cash Fund at the beginning of the year, to be replenished as soon as
disbursements reach 75%, to be fully liquidated at the end of the year.
The amount shall be sufficient for the non-recurring, emergency and
petty expenses of the LGU for one month.

b. Only petty expenses shall be paid from said cash advance through the use of
the Petty Cash Voucher (PCV). Regular expenses such as rentals,
subscriptions, utilities shall not be paid from the PCF.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

During the exit conference, management agreed with the audit recommendations
and committed to comply accordingly.

RECOMMENDATIONS

We recommended that:

a. The City Mayor create a Disposal Committee who shall take charge of the
disposal of the unserviceable properties, following existing guidelines
such as COA Circular No. 92-386 (Rules and Regulations on Supply and
Property Management in the Local Government); and

b. The General Services Officer prepare the Inventory and Inspection
Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) using the NGAS-prescribed
format so that important information such as the cost thereof will be
indicated.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

Management concurred with the audit recommendation and committed during the
exit conference that they will come up with a time frame to formulate the required 10-
year plan in accordance with R.A. 9003.

RECOMMENDATION

We recommended that management set a time frame for the City’s SWM
Board to formulate the new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan in
accordance with the provisions of R.A. 9003 in order to maximize the pervasive
benefits forthwith considering the length of time that has lapsed since the expiration
of the city’s 2002 ISWM Plan.

MANAGEMENT COMMENT

Management agreed with the audit recommendations and committed to comply
accordingly.

RECOMMENDATIONS

We recommended that management improve the implementation of Solid
Waste Management pursuant to R.A. 9003 through the following:

a) Adopt a viable and effective collection schedule for specific types of
wastes to be collected;

b) Acquire additional garbage trucks to implement waste segregation at
source and point of collection;

c) Widen the coverage of the city’s garbage collection system; and

d) Require the SWM Board to strengthen their Information, Education and
Communication (IEC) Program to increase the awareness of the
constituents on waste management.

We reiterate our CY 2016 recommendation that: a. The City Accountant submit to the audit team on 12/01/2017 and 01/04/2018: Assistant City Accountant 0911-0993-57 June 2008–Aug 2017 had to conduct an inventory of the vital documents available (bank statements, subsidiary ledgers, journals, financial statements, passbooks, BRS – for those that were not available).
observations.managementAction: a. The following monthly BRS were submitted to the audit team on 12/01/2017 and 01/04/2018: 0911-0993-57 June 2008–Aug 2017. 1. Documents needed to conduct bank reconciliation were not intact nor complete. When the Assistant City Accountant assumed on April 18, 2017 she had to conduct an inventory of the vital documents available (bank statements, subsidiary ledgers, journals, financial statements, passbooks, BRS – for those that were not available). 2. The City Mayor hired an Assistant City Accountant to assist the City Accountant in the preparation of BRS and in the day-to-day operations of the Office of the City Accountant.
observations.reason: Documents needed to conduct bank reconciliation were not intact nor complete. When the Assistant City Accountant assumed on April 18, 2017 she had to conduct an inventory of the vital documents available (bank statements, subsidiary ledgers, journals, financial statements, passbooks, BRS – for those that were not available). The Assistant City Accountant was also designated as Tourism Officer and had to perform additional functions which took much of her time – checking and signing of disbursement vouchers and other documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our CY 2016 recommendation for the local chief executive to require the conduct of the physical inventory count and submission of a report thereon to the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Results of the annual physical inventory count should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and the necessary adjustments made so that both records agree with the actual count and the related accounts presented fairly in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The City considered the residual value of all items of property, plant and equipment, but is not expecting residual values for these assets. These assets will be utilized for their entire economic lives with no significant scrap value. During the current financial year, the LGU reviewed the estimated useful lives and residual values of property, plant and equipment, where appropriate. Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) are being maintained for most accounts. 100% compliance is targeted for 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a) Require the City Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and update these monthly using the reports submitted by the City Agriculturist, and require, as well, the GSO to maintain property cards as reference for identifying the animals; b) Require the City Accountant to reconcile the records on work/zoo animals and breeding stocks with those maintained by the City Agriculturist, and draw the JEV for the reported birth of offspring, or drop from the record in case of death, transfer or sale of work animals/breeding stocks that are traceable and are part of the originally recorded work animals/breeding stocks; and c) Henceforth, require the City Agriculturist to prepare a report within two days from the birth of animals. In the case of death of offspring, the City Agriculturist shall likewise submit a report within two days stating the value of the animal, causes/reasons of death and other necessary details. Likewise, a Monthly Report of Inventory shall be prepared for all animals within 10 days after the end of the month, copy furnished the GSO and the City Accountant.
observations.managementAction: CY 2016 Livestock ledgers are now being prepared by the City Accountant. The City Agriculturist has yet to submit reports on births, deaths and dispositions of livestock to the City Accountant. She is still trying to come up with formats of required reports.
observations.reason: Records of assets, particularly those acquired in previous years, could not be located/produced.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a. The Sangguniang Panlungsod institute internal guidelines in the purchase and assignment of government-issued telephones, and the charging of mobile prepaid load cards and postpaid plan subscriptions to government funds, and to consider as well the limits as to rank, cost of cellphone, number of units per official (only one) and amount of postpaid plan as assets paid portion of the cellular phones be amortized for a period of 2 years; b. Management disapprove charging of add-ons; c. City officials up to department head level who were issued or holding multiple unit each return the excess and keep only one unit while employees below department head level who are not entitled to the privilege be required to return their units to the General Services Office for disposal through auction sale; and d. Management disapprove the charging to LGU funds unnecessary and unconscionable mobile telephone expenses.
observations.managementAction: a. The Sangguniang Panlungsod passed Ordinance No. 2017-25 prescribing the guidelines in the issuing of telephone units and the charging of mobile prepaid plans on November 28, 2017. b. The cellular phones that come from the postpaid subscriptions be recorded in the books as government assets and covered by Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) upon issuance to the end-users; c. Department Heads availing of postpaid plans now only have 1 unit. d. Management disapproves charging of add-ons.
observations.reason: The costs of cellular phones are spread throughout the lock-periods, thus, the monthly amortizations do not reach the threshold of P15,000.00 for asset capitalization. Some documents needed for application of ARE and booking as assets were not yet available. The City Accountant now requires the presentation or attachment of Requisition and Issue Slips to support the charging of purchases as well as proper Requisition and Issue Slips. This is in accordance with PAS 17 and PAS 36.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management take immediate action on the unfinished and unimplemented projects so that the constituents can benefit therefrom at the soonest possible time and, henceforth, comply with the provisions of Section 287 of RA 7160, or the Local Government Code of 1991 and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the implementation of priority development projects. We further recommend that management provide the City Engineering Office with enough personnel to ensure timely completion of programmed projects.
observations.managementAction: Some ongoing projects in prior years were completed in 2017. The City is reviewing the budget for 2018 for possible re-appropriation of funds intended for development projects which can no longer be implemented. HRMO promised to hire additional personnel once the election ban has lapsed.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize the immediate implementation of the projects/programs/activities funded by the identified programs and projects of the CY 2015 Bottom-up Budgeting Program (PPAs).
observations.managementAction: The Upgrading of Waterworks System had commenced. P3,500,000.00 DILG - Upgrading of Waterworks – 50% implemented as of 12/31/2017. P700,000.00 DA - Marine Reserve and Fish Sanctuary - Bidding conducted on 12/27/2017 but project awarded on 02/16/2018. P2,000,000.00 DILG – Construction of Flood Dike – Bidding conducted on May 31, 2017 and was awarded July 3, 2017. Accomplishment as of 12/31/2017 was 20% but no payment has yet been made.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the City Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from BAC/BOD honoraria and that the necessary supporting documents and appropriate official receipts are obtained; b. Submit the necessary supporting documents to validate the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: In the payment of honoraria of the BAC Chair and members as of August 2017, withholding taxes amounting to P39,094.94 were deducted from the total amount of P148,000.00. Accounting adjustments were made and remittances effected to BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Secure from the concerned suppliers/service providers the required warranty certificates on repairs and maintenance of vehicles and equipment; b. Require the GSO to maintain records of the history of repairs on equipment and vehicles and submit the same to the auditor when repairs and maintenance is undertaken; and c. Henceforth, ensure the completeness of the supporting documents attached to the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs).
observations.managementAction: The GSO is in the process of accounting and safekeeping of all records. Records of repairs and maintenance are now being located. The City is now ensuring submission of repair documents.
observations.reason: Documents for repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles could not be located/produced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly reports together with trial balances within the reglementary period; otherwise, cause the suspension of salaries of responsible officials until they shall have submitted the required documents and reports.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant is coordinating with the State Auditor on the prompt submission of the requested reports and trial balances. The City Accountant requested creation/funding of 7 plantilla positions.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower. Preparedness ongoing; some required reports were submitted and coordination with the State Auditor is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a) Concretize an area within a specific time frame and endeavor to operate a sanitary landfill as the final disposal site for solid wastes, designed and operated in accordance with RA 9003; b) Set a time frame for the City Solid Waste Management Board to formulate a new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan incorporating specific guidelines on waste segregation at source and the establishment of collection systems for segregated solid waste; and c) Require the SWM Board to strengthen their IEC Program to increase awareness of constituents on waste management.
observations.managementAction: The City acquired a lot in Barangay Basak containing 104,194 square meters as evidenced by TCT No. 2017002286. Plans to improve the existing dumpsite and develop the lot as sanitary landfill are incorporated in the Work and Financial Plan for 2018.
observations.reason: Time constraint.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that henceforth, under Section 5.b.3 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 8185 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1, management strictly observe the provisions on the proper utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The fire truck costing P7,549,999.00 is now booked under Property, Plant and Equipment – Motor Vehicles. Amount was also restored as part of 70% LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management create a GAD M&E Team who shall evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects pursuant to Item 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: An Executive Order creating the GAD M&E Team was already issued, in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the City Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from BAC honoraria in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table and remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period; and b. Submit the lacking supporting documents to establish the validity of the payments.
observations.managementAction: Withholding taxes were deducted from the honoraria and remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period. Adjustments were effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management give priority to the inventory of its insurable properties and insure the same with the GIF of the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: A number of city properties, including the Mega Market, are insured with the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS. The City is still identifying assets to insure. Application documents are being prepared. City Assessor's office was tasked for listing.
observations.reason: Application documents needed for insurance application were not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Assessor to prepare the SMV pursuant to Section 212 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and cause the passage of an ordinance incorporating therein the proposed SMV as basis for the conduct of a general revision of real property assessments.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the City Assessor prepared an SMV using the 1998 Revised Schedule of Market Values of the Province of Negros Oriental. The SMV has yet to be approved by the Regional Office.
observations.reason: The City Assessor who was the only licensed appraiser of the office retired in 2017. The SMV prepared by the office still has to be approved by the Regional Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from granting cash advances or incentives to JO workers who are not government employees pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 02-1480, and henceforth stop granting cash advances to non-government employees.
observations.managementAction: Travel advances were refunded in full as of December 29, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending government funds for events that do not benefit the City Government and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure to immediately refund the amount disbursed upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance to be issued by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Payments of the questioned expenses were refunded upon notice and some personnel have been held administratively liable and separated from the service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to review all balances dormant in the Trust Fund books for two (2) years to more than ten (10) years and identify excess balances for projects. Once ascertained, return the excess to source agency. Further, prepare the necessary journal entries to close the projects and transfer these to the LGU’s General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: Review of accounts ongoing. Some funds ongoing for liquidation. Identified dormant balances reduced. As a result of intensive collection efforts, unliquidated cash advances have been reduced to P1,830,194.35 as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: Ongoing review. Lack of manpower delayed full implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended issuance of final demand letters to concerned accountable officers and employees, and impose sanctions including withholding of salaries to collect unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Final demand letters were issued to concerned officers and employees. As a result of intensive collection efforts, unliquidated cash advances have been reduced to P1,830,194.35 as of December 31, 2017, or 91.20% decrease.
observations.reason: Some accountable personnel are deceased or separated; collection continues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City require all city officials and employees to refund the overpayment and observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI; and amounts collected to be used to reduce the balance of refunds.
observations.managementAction: P500.00 is collected from each official and employee every month. Amount collected as of 12/31/2017 is P1,153,720.90 bringing the balance to P5,897,779.10, or a 16% decrease. Outstanding balance is being deducted from terminal leave benefits upon retirement/separation from service. Some officials and employees have been separated from service.
observations.reason: Availability of personal funds of some officials/employees allowed partial recovery; process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Require the City Assessor and Treasurer to update the data/information on Real Property Tax Assessment Roll and furnish the City Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers containing taxes due and collectible as basis for recording the Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year as required in Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; b. Require the City Accountant to recognize receivables from RPT/SET at the beginning of each year on the basis of the Certified List of Taxpayers and Amounts Due; c. Require the City Accountant to record collections of RPT/SET using the prescribed entries; d. Require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to conduct reconciliation of records; and e. Require the City Treasurer to conduct information campaigns and employ civil remedies as necessary.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and City Assessor have received the List of Real Property Tax Receivables from the City Treasurer in 2018 to be used as the beginning balance of Receivables for 2018. The City Accountant is presently using the Integrated Real Property Taxation System. A massive clean-up was conducted and Compromise Agreement Ordinance was approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod to assist delinquent taxpayers. Statements of Delinquency and Notices are being sent.
observations.reason: Some landowners were unable to present required documents; some properties still need titling and verification; process ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. Stop the practice of directly charging purchases to expense and instead implement the prescribed procedures for the purchase and utilization of supplies and materials pursuant to Sections 51, 114, 116 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs; b. Require end-user departments to prepare RIS for submission to the GSO on a weekly basis; c. Require the GSO to consolidate the SSMI and submit to the Accountant weekly; and d. Require the Accountant to record to the expense accounts the supplies consumed on the basis of the SSMI and to maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with GSO records.
observations.managementAction: The City is now requiring the presentation or attachment of Requisition and Issue Slips to support the charging of purchases as well as proper Requisition and Issue Slips. The City Accountant is recording purchases properly and requiring SSMIs for expense and inventory recording. Supplies of less than P15,000.00 are charged to expense as allowed under policy.
observations.reason: Some supporting documentation and SSMI consolidation were not in place due to prior practice; improvement ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office require the submission of the monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRM funds to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 15th day after the end of each month to facilitate review and monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO is now coordinating with the City Accountant to ensure prompt submission of DRRM reports.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower in the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the city close the PNB accounts and transfer the deposits to LBP-Guihulngan Branch and reconcile balances per bank and books based on Bank Reconciliation Statements.
observations.managementAction: As a result of audit, the City coordinated for transfer of the deposits to LBP-Guihulngan Branch. Total outstanding balance of PNB was cut down to P4,957,666.78 as of 12/31/2017. The PNB accounts are to be closed upon reconciliation of balances per bank and books.
observations.reason: Bank reconciliation statements for the PNB accounts were not yet available as of 12/31/2017, precluding the transfer to LBP-Guihulngan Branch.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Offices of the City Mayor and Sangguniang Panlungsod: a) Stop the hiring of JOs and charging their wages to appropriations for legislative research which act is illegal and punishable by law; b) Submit a written explanation or justification establishing the necessity of hiring 466 JOs for the period January to June 2013 coinciding with the election ban period; c) Furnish the audit team with the output of the legislative research project such as newly approved/drafts of ordinances and resolutions based from the researches gathered by the 466 JOs hired; and d) Henceforth, strictly comply with Section 336 of the Local Government Code and Article VI, Section 29 of the Philippine Constitution which require that funds shall be used only for the specific purpose for which it was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: The City discontinued the hiring of similar JOs in subsequent years and coordinated with HRMO to regularize plantilla positions. Some outputs were submitted. The Mayor's Office and SP were asked to justify the hiring during the election ban period.
observations.reason: Output of the legislative research project was not submitted to the audit team in a consolidated manner; some justifications were provided but full documentation lacking.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Prior to any repair and requisition of spare parts, pre-repair inspection reports must be conducted by the Maintenance and Engineering department and documented in a Pre-Repair Evaluation Report; b) Pre-repair inspection be documented in a Pre-Repair Inspection Report to provide precise assessment of the repair required and establish necessity of repairs and spare parts; c) Requisitions must be prepared by the head of office or department and Waste Material Reports signed by the person accountable; and d) All deliveries of items purchased must first be received by the GSO.
observations.managementAction: Procedures now in place. The GSO is maintaining records of repairs and pre-repair inspection reports. Some required forms are being filled and attached to DVs for post-audit.
observations.reason: Some required pre-repair inspection reports and waste material reports were still lacking for earlier transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) The city’s fuel consumption be properly and completely controlled and regulated through the issuance of properly and completely accomplished serially numbered driver’s trip tickets duly approved by the Administrative Officer of each department; b) Drivers be required to prepare Monthly Reports of Official Travel summarizing the trip tickets of their respective vehicles, to be approved by the Administrative Officer; c) The GSO be required to prepare the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor for verification purposes; and d) The Accountant should process claims for payment of gasoline and oil only if completely supported with complete documentation; Acceptance and Inspection Report must be signed by the GSO/Supply Officer as the officer receiving the delivery.
observations.managementAction: The City Administrator is now requiring drivers to properly and completely accomplish serially numbered trip tickets. The City Accountant now requires the drivers to prepare monthly trip ticket summary reports. The GSO prepares monthly fuel consumption reports.
observations.reason: Due to high volume of trip tickets being attached to disbursement vouchers, there is a possibility that the City Accountant would not be able to notice some deficiencies; full compliance is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. The personnel responsible for the processing of the payments for gasoline and oil require the conduct of inspection of items delivered to ensure completeness and compliance by the inspection team – the GSO, ICU and Accountant’s Office; b. The City Accountant and accounting personnel in charge of processing payments for fuel be instructed to approve payment for just the quantity actually delivered; c. The gasoline station supplier be required to restitute the amount of overpayment; and d. The Accountant must require complete supporting documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Demand was already issued to the concerned supplier. The City Accountant now requires the conduct of inspection of items delivered to ensure completeness and compliance.
observations.reason: Refund from supplier was not yet received as of report; follow up ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a) Conduct a physical inventory of all involved properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets; b) Require that properties under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the specific account of the general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements and depreciation be recognized annually; and c) Require the LGU to maintain a properly accomplished/updated PPELC for easy verification and reference both by the auditee and the auditor.
observations.managementAction: The City is in the process of identifying and liquidating funds from various agencies. Identified completed projects shall be transferred to the General Fund under Property, Plant and Equipment. PPE Ledger Cards are being maintained for most accounts. 100% compliance targeted for 2018.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower, and some downloaded funds still have remaining balances, thus projects were not yet closed in the books and transferred to PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate approval of its staffing pattern and immediately employ or appoint an educationally-qualified and properly motivated person to discharge the functions of a General Services Officer in pursuance of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: The creation of the position was approved on September 14, 2016 and Gian Carlo A. Mijares has been designated as Acting GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management coordinate with the department heads concerned to identify the assets and liabilities and prepare the necessary adjusting entries so that the financial statements can be fairly presented; and ensure a proper physical inventory of assets.
observations.managementAction: Liabilities were cut down to P600,000.00 while unidentified assets and liabilities are still not fully identified and the required physical inventory of assets was not completed in 2017 due to lack of manpower. The City is in the process of reconciling and validating accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower; required physical inventory of assets could not be conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management consider disposing of its unserviceable properties within the year or the soonest possible time in order to realize additional income otherwise these unserviceable properties may continue to deteriorate and eventually lose their salvage values. Management is further advised to be guided by Chapter 6 of the COA Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System in the disposal process.
observations.managementAction: The GSO is in the process of accounting and safekeeping of all assets. Identified unserviceable properties are being listed for disposal through the prescribed process. Some were prepared for disposal.
observations.reason: Lack of storage space and manpower to handle safekeeping and disposal processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City thoroughly review the validity of those outstanding payables accounts, remit to individuals/agencies concerned those payables found to be valid, and request for the reversion back to surplus all those which have remained outstanding in the books for more than two years and for which no administrative or judicial claim has been filed. Further, ensure strict compliance with regulations in recognition and recording of liabilities.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant promised to conduct a thorough review of all long-outstanding payables and initiate processes to remit and/or revert unsubstantiated balances to surplus. Partial settlements were effected where claims were valid.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower; some payables require documentation to validate and remit.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Treasurer’s Office, Accounting Office and the City Assessor’s Office to reconcile their records on real properties and maintain proper subsidiary ledgers and exert extra effort to prioritize the filing of applications for titling of all real properties acquired by the city government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the City of Guihulngan can be issued to establish the city’s ownership thereto.
observations.managementAction: The City Treasurer and City Assessor coordinated and started actions to exert extra effort to prioritize the filing of necessary documents for titling and preparation of subsidiary ledgers. TCT application efforts were initiated and filed for certain properties.
observations.reason: Some properties still lack documentary evidence for titling; efforts ongoing to secure documentation and prepare subsidiary ledgers in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Assessor prepare the SMV pursuant to Section 212 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and incorporate therein the proposed SMV as basis for the conduct of a general revision of real property assessments in adherence to Section 219 of the same Code and DILG-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2010-001.
observations.managementAction: The City Assessor’s personnel are scheduled to attend seminar training on Inspection and Property Valuation with the Department of Finance, to aide them in the preparation of the SMV.
observations.reason: The City is still implementing the Revised Schedule of Market Values of the Province of Negros Oriental. General revision of real property assessment as a city is yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required documents for the procurement and distribution of subject emergency items, require the CDRRMO and City Accountant to account for the distribution by submitting to the audit team the distribution lists with addresses of beneficiaries and the description and quantity of items given per bag which should reconcile with the total goods purchased; submit a written explanation regarding inconsistencies between date of distribution and date of acceptance and inspection; and require the City Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Lacking documents required for procurement and distribution were not submitted to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the City Accountant to review all balances specifically those which remained dormant in the Trust Fund books for two (2) years to more than ten (10) years and identify excess balances for projects. Once ascertained, return excess to source agency. Further, prepare necessary journal entries to close the trust fund liabilities under Due to Other NGAs and transfer these projects to the LGU's General Fund books.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Dormant accounts are still subject for verification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require all city officials and employees to refund the overpayment of Productivity Enhancement Incentive for CY 2014 of P7,051,500.00 and henceforth observe strictly the limitations on the grant of PEI.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: No refund was made in 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit the required certifications from PS-DBM to support the validity of the disbursements, and henceforth observe strictly the policies and procedures under the revised IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement of common-use supplies.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Certifications from PS-DBM of the subject disbursements were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: Require the City Accountant to review and investigate the difference by tracing the erroneous journal entries, prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reconcile the Due To/From Other Funds accounts in the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund books and henceforth regularly reconcile these two reciprocal accounts.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Difficulty in tracing past transactions of the Due To/From Other Funds account.

observations.recommendation: Conduct physical inventory counts of all properties to determine the existence and physical condition of these assets; require that properties, plant and equipment under the trust fund be immediately transferred to the general fund for proper presentation of the financial statements pursuant to Section 104 of NGAS; require the LGU to maintain properly accomplished/updated PPE Ledger Cards.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Physical inventory counts of all properties were not conducted; properties remain recorded in the Trust Fund; no transfer to General Fund was made; PPELC not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management find ways and means to utilize and ensure proper maintenance of the slaughterhouse to prevent deterioration and avoid incurrence of high cost of repair.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The said slaughterhouse is under investigation with the Ombudsman and remains unutilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended disposing of unserviceable properties through public bidding to realize additional income and to be guided by COA Training Handbook on Property and Supply Management System in the disposal process.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Physical disposition through public bidding not conducted; properties still deteriorating.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Assessor to exert extra effort to prioritize the filing of application for titling of all real properties acquired by the city government so that transfer certificates of title in favor of the City of Guihulngan can be issued to establish the city’s ownership; and maintain subsidiary records for land account.
observations.managementAction: None
