observations.reason: No available records in the Accounting Office; titling and subsidiary records not completed.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that:
a. The City Accountant submit to the auditor the monthly BRS from
1999 to the present, otherwise the sanctions provided under Section
3.9 of the aforementioned COA Circular, which is to recommend the
filing of administrative charges against those responsible for the
delayed submission thereof, shall be imposed; and
b. The City Mayor assign competent personnel to concentrate on the
preparation of the BRS.

We reiterated our recommendation for the local chief executive to
require the conduct of the physical inventory count and the submission of a
report thereon to the audit team. Results of the annual physical inventory
should be reconciled with the accounting and property records and the
necessary adjustments made so that both records agree with the actual count
in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended further that the local chief executive instruct the
Inventory Committee to plan and strategize the conduct of the physical count
of properties every year, giving emphasis to accountability for such
properties and the sanctions provided under Sections 122 and 127 of P.D.
1445.

We recommended that management:
a) Require the City Accountant to maintain livestock ledger cards and to
update these monthly using the reports submitted by the City
Agriculturist, and require, as well, the GSO to maintain property
cards as reference for identifying each animal in the conduct of
physical inventory as well as for reconciliation with accounting
records;
b) Require the City Accountant to reconcile the records on work/zoo
animals and breeding stocks with those maintained by the City
Agriculturist, and draw the JEV for the reported birth of offspring,
or drop from the record in case of death, transfer or sale of work
animals/breeding stocks that are traceable and are part of the
originally recorded work animals/breeding stocks; and
c) Henceforth, require the City Agriculturist to prepare a report within
two days from the birth of animals. In the case of death of offspring,
the City Agriculturist shall likewise submit a report within two days
stating the value of the animal, causes/reasons of death and other
necessary details. Likewise, a Monthly Report of Inventory shall be
prepared for all animals within 10 days after the end of the month,
copy furnished the GSO and the City Accountant.

We recommended that:
a. The Sangguniang Panlungsod institute internal rules to regulate the
purchase and assignment of cellular phones and the charging of
mobile telephone expenses, particularly postpaid plan subscriptions,
to government funds, and to consider as well the limits as to rank, cost
of cellphone, number of units per official (only one) and amount of
postpaid plan expenses chargeable to government funds;
b. The cellular phones that come from the postpaid subscriptions be
recorded in the books as government assets and covered by
Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) upon issuance to the
end-users;
c. City officials up to department head level who were issued or holding
multiple cellphones either from requisition or postpaid plan to return
the excess and keep only one unit while employees below department
head level who are not entitled to the privilege be required to return
their units to the General Services Office for disposal through auction
sale; and
d. Management disapprove the charging to LGU funds unnecessary and
unconscionable mobile telephone expenses such as monthly
amortization/one time charge for cellphone upgrade, additional
subscription on top of existing ones, Gadget Care and Share a Load,
and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary
expenditure to immediately refund the amount disbursed upon receipt
of the Notice of Disallowance which shall be issued by the audit team.

We recommended that management take immediate action on the
unfinished and unimplemented projects so that the constituents can benefit
therefrom at the soonest possible time and, henceforth, comply with the
provisions of Section 287 of RA 7160, or the Local Government Code of 1991,
and the related DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the
prompt and full utilization of the 20% Development Fund for the
implementation of priority development projects in order to optimally attain
the socio-economic and environmental development of the City Government.

We further recommended that management provide the City
Engineering Office with enough personnel to ensure timely completion of
programmed projects.

We recommended that management prioritize the immediate
implementation of the projects/programs/activities funded by the National
Government in order not to delay further the benefits expected from the
implementation of the identified programs, projects and activities (PPAs).

We recommended that management demand from the treasurers of
the various leagues of LGUs and local elective officials the acknowledgment
of amounts paid to them with official receipts of the Republic of the
Philippines (Accountable Form No. 51) in order that the funds will be
disbursed for official purposes and subjected to government accounting and
auditing rules and regulations.

We recommended that management:
a. Secure from concerned suppliers/service providers the required
warranty certificates on disbursements for repairs and maintenance
of equipment, vehicles and others of similar nature as required under
the aforementioned COA Circular;
b. Require the GSO to maintain records of the history of repairs of
equipment and vehicles and submit the same to the auditor when
repairs and maintenance is undertaken; and
c. Henceforth, ensure the completeness of the supporting documents
attached to the DVs.

We recommended that management require the City Accountant to
prepare and submit the monthly reports and trial balances together with
schedules to the auditor within the reglementary period. Otherwise, cause
the suspension of salaries of responsible officials until they shall have
submitted the required documents and reports pursuant to Section 122 of
P.D. 1445.

We recommended that management:
a) Concretize methods and procedures for the phase-out and the
eventual closure of the existing open dumpsite within a specific time
frame;
b) Endeavor to operate a sanitary landfill as final disposal site for solid
wastes. This landfill should be designed and operated in accordance
with the guidelines/criteria mentioned under RA 9003;
c) Set a time frame for the City Solid Waste Management Board to
formulate a new 10-Year Integrated Solid Waste Management Plan
incorporating therewith specific guidelines on waste segregation at
source and the establishment of collection systems for segregated solid
waste in order to promote recycling and re-use of resources and to
reduce the volume of waste for final disposal; and
d) Require the SWM Board to strengthen their Information, Education
and Communication (IEC) Program to increase the awareness of the
constituents on waste management.

We recommended that management appropriate in the General Fund
the amount to cover the cost of the fire truck in order that the LDRRMF will
be restored. We further recommended that henceforth, management strictly
observe the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1 and the Rules and Regulations Implementing R.A. 8185
on the proper utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that management create a GAD M&E Team who
shall evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects
pursuant to Item 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management:
a. Require the City Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are
deducted from BAC honoraria in accordance with the Revised
Withholding Tax Table and to remit the same to the BIR within the
prescribed period; and
b. Submit the lacking supporting documents to support the subject
disbursements and, henceforth, to ensure the completeness and
validity of the supporting documents attached to the payrolls.

We recommend that the City conduct an AAR inventory of its insurable properties giving priority to high value items, appropriate the necessary premiums in its annual budget under the General Insurance Fund and insure the same with the GIF of the GSIS every year.
observations.managementAction: Only the newly constructed Mega Market was insured with the GIF of the GSIS during the year.
observations.reason: Due to lack of necessary documents needed for insurance application.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LSB prepare the required Annual SEF Budget pursuant to Section 99(a) of R.A. 7160 and utilize the same in accordance with the prioritized programs as provided under Section 100(c) and 272 of the same law.
observations.managementAction: Annual SEF Budget for CY 2016 was prepared and approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the officials who authorized the grant of said cash advances to refund the total amount disbursed. We further recommended that management desist from granting cash advances to job order workers who are not considered government employees pursuant to CSC Resolution No. 02-1480 and COA Circular No. 97-002, otherwise, these shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Demands for the settlement of cash advances were sent to the concerned accountable officers and employees and after which impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of grantees as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.reason: Refund was not yet made by concerned city officials who authorized the grant of subject cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from spending the city’s funds which do not benefit the City Government but only a few private individuals and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure of P1,785,124.54 to immediately refund, unless appealed, the amount disbursed upon receipt of the Notice of Disallowance which shall be issued by the audit team.
observations.managementAction: Management has desisted from spending the city’s funds. Demands for the refund of the irregular expenditures were issued.
observations.reason: Refund was not immediately made pending issuance of Notice of Disallowance by the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management desist from charging such purchases to government funds and require the persons responsible for the irregular and unnecessary expenditure of P615,400.00 to refund the amount disbursed; further recommend the City Mayor stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts pursuant to Section 54.1 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The City’s Charter Day shirts were no longer charged to government funds. The City Mayor was advised to stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions and contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Health Officer ensure that drugs and medicines requisitioned are included in the PNDF Volume 1, current edition and issue a certification to that effect. Henceforth, the city officials should adhere strictly to the provisions of AO No. 163 in the procurement of drugs and medicines.
observations.managementAction: The City complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management limit the incurrence of expenses for meals and snacks during meetings with constituents and special occasions to the amount of the appropriation and require the persons responsible to refund the irregular expenditures, stop the practice of serving meals and snacks to a big crowd of spectators/listeners of campaign related events and the charging thereof to government funds, submit the required PRs to support the validity of subject disbursements and ensure completeness and validity of supporting documents attached to the DVs.
observations.managementAction: The City complied with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management formulate a GAD Plan which conforms to Joint Circular No. 2012-01 of the PCW, NEDA and DBM, and to consider only those programs/activities/projects which are gender-related.
observations.managementAction: The city’s programs and projects that are gender responsive are now attributed in the GAD Plan and GAD Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City prepare and submit the annual GAD Accomplishment Report following the form prescribed in Annex B of Item 5.2 of Joint Circular No. 2004-01 so that review and evaluation of the same is facilitated.
observations.managementAction: GAD Accomplishment Reports for CY 2015 and 2016 were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management enforce settlement of cash advances by issuing final demand letters to the concerned accountable officers and employees and after which impose the sanction of withholding the salaries of grantees as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Demands for the settlement of cash advances were sent to the concerned officers and employees. Pending credit advice from the COA Chairman. Unliquidated cash advances (Account Due from officers and employees) as of December 31, 2016 comprised mainly of the confidential and intelligence fund of the City Mayor now Vice Mayor.
observations.reason: Unliquidated cash advances pending credit advice from the COA Chairman; refunds not fully made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Assessor and Treasurer update data/information on Real Property Tax and furnish the City Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers containing taxes due and payable as basis for recording Real Property/Special Education Tax Receivables at the beginning of the year; and require the Office of the City Treasurer to conduct a massive tax delinquency clean up drive to remove exempted/demolished/cancelled delinquent properties in the billing system.
observations.managementAction: The city has complied with the audit recommendations except letter "b".
observations.reason: No entry was made to set-up RPT and SEF at the beginning of the year, as per verification of the general ledgers of General Fund and SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging purchases directly to expense as simultaneous recording of inventory and expense; require end-user departments to prepare RIS for utilization and submit to GSO weekly; require GSO to consolidate SSMI and submit to the accountant weekly; require the accountant to record to expense accounts the supplies consumed on the basis of SSMI and maintain perpetual inventory records to be reconciled periodically with GSO records.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of inventory items are now being recorded to the inventory account as simultaneous recording of inventory and expense accounts is being implemented.
observations.reason: Post audit of related disbursements showed that there are still inventory items that were recorded to expense without issuance slips to inventory and utilization procedures were not fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer submit the monthly report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to facilitate review and monitoring of disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Only the Annual Report on Utilization of LDRRMF was prepared and submitted by the City Accountant.
observations.reason: Monthly reports were not submitted by the City DRRM Office during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer transfer the city’s deposits with PNB-Guihulngan Branch amounting to P110,814,414.16 to the LBP-Guihulngan Branch.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of funds to LBP-Guihulngan is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Bank Confirmation as of December 31, 2016 showed that deposit amounting to P8,641,502.93 was still maintained in PNB-Guihulngan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Offices of the Mayor and Sangguniang Panlungsod stop the hiring of JOs and charging their wages to appropriations for legislative research, submit justification for the hiring and furnish the audit team with the output of the legislative research project such as newly approved/drafts of ordinances and resolutions based from the researches gathered by the 466 JOs, and henceforth strictly comply with Section 336 of the Local Government Code and Article VI, Section 29 of the Philippine Constitution which require funds to be used only for the specific purpose for which it was appropriated.
observations.managementAction: The city has complied with the audit recommendations except letter "c".
observations.reason: Approved/drafts of ordinances and researches gathered by the 466 JOs were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor and Sangguniang Panlungsod justify the hiring of additional job order employees; henceforth, wages of contractual/job order workers hired through piece work shall be charged to the project under the appropriate MOOE or Capital Outlay account; the City Accountant should ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: Transfer of wages and hiring practices are being monitored and adjustments made as required.
observations.reason: Some late adjustments were made; 466 JOs have been regularized or monitored, but full compliance ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that prior to any repair and requisition of spare parts, pre-repair inspection must be supported and documented in a Pre-Repair Inspection Report; pre-repair inspection conducted by authorized personnel shall be documented; requisitions must be prepared by head of office and Waste Material Reports signed by the person accountable; all deliveries of items purchased must first be received by the GSO.
observations.managementAction: The Pre and Post inspection reports are now attached to the DVs while waste reports are now signed by the administrative officer.
observations.reason: Post-audit showed there were some spare parts payments not supported with required pre-repair inspection reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the city’s fuel consumption should be properly controlled and regulated through the issuance of serially numbered trip tickets, require drivers to properly accomplish trip tickets and for Administrative Officer to approve; General Services Office be required to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and submit to the Office of the Auditor; the Accountant should process claims only if completely supported; Acceptance and Inspection Report must be signed by GSO/Supply Officer.
observations.managementAction: The city is requiring the drivers to properly accomplish the trip tickets.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels were not consistently submitted to the Auditor; some trip tickets remained incomplete.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that personnel responsible be required to restitute the amount; and that City Accountant approve payment for just the quantity actually delivered.
observations.managementAction: Demand was already issued to the concerned suppliers.
observations.reason: To date, no refund was received from the suppliers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor designate a responsible personnel under the GSO to record and monitor the issuance of drugs and medicines to indigents and counter-check eligibility documents; GSO to require a duly-approved Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) as basis for issuing drugs; City Social Welfare and Development Officer to maintain an electronic database for indigents; City Health Officer to prepare Certification of conformity with PNDF for every purchase; City Accountant should record purchases to inventory account and maintain ledger cards to be reconciled with GSO stock cards.
observations.managementAction: The city complied with the audit recommendations; pre and post inspection reports are now attached and certification requirements being followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management facilitate the approval of its staffing pattern and appoint an educationally-qualified and properly motivated person to discharge the functions of a General Services Officer.
observations.managementAction: The creation of the position of General Services Officer has already been approved last September 14, 2016.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the General Services Officer has been appointed but full effect implementation ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with department heads to identify the assets and liabilities and conduct the required physical inventory and submit the required report which should be the basis for necessary adjustment entries.
observations.managementAction: Liabilities were cut down to P600,000.00 while unidentified assets remained unresolved due to failure to conduct the required physical inventory count.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee still failed to conduct a physical inventory count of properties and submit the required report which should have been the basis for adjustment entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant thoroughly review the validity of those long outstanding payable accounts, remit to individuals/agencies concerned like the GSIS, PAG-IBIG, other LGUs, and request for reversion back to surplus all those which have remained outstanding in the books for more than two years and for which no administrative or judicial claim has been filed. Further, strict compliance with regulations in recognition and recording of liabilities is required.
observations.managementAction: Unpaid obligations are now being closely monitored by the City Accountant.
observations.reason: Obligations made prior to 1999 may no longer represent valid claims; ongoing review.
